confirmed.area: console list actions posting un-hydrated sales
confirmed.claim: Actions post raw includeDetails=false list rows (Order.OrderPackages always [] per gba-server SalesController.cs:939) to update endpoints: requestWillNotShip at SalesUkrainePage.tsx:604 posts updateSale({...sale, IsAcceptedToPacking:true}) and /sales/update runs RemoveAllByOrderId when posted packages are empty (SalesActor.cs:670); SaleDetailsDrawer opened un-hydrated via onOpenDetails=setDetailsSale (SalesUkrainePage.tsx:958) saves {...sale} via /sales/update/file with the identical wipe path (SalesActor.cs:1082), and its carrier change-history table (SaleDetailsDrawer.tsx:342) reads sale.UpdateDataCarrier which the list projection hardcodes to [] (SalesController.cs:1015) so history is permanently empty.
confirmed.expected: Hydrate via ensureSaleDetails before posting or opening the drawer (as openSaleSheet/openSaleDiscount do), or omit Order from the payload; legacy posts the includeDetails=true sale whose real OrderPackages survive (SaleRepository.cs:2949+).
confirmed.explanation: CONFIRMED end-to-end. The console sales list is fetched with includeDetails=false (salesUkraineApi.ts:32) and the server summary projection returns Order.OrderPackages=[] and UpdateDataCarrier=[] (SalesController.cs:938-941, 1015). Two console actions post these un-hydrated rows back: (1) requestWillNotShip (SalesUkrainePage.tsx:604) posts {...sale, IsAcceptedToPacking:true} to /sales/update, where ProcessUpdateSaleMessage sees Order!=null with empty OrderPackages and executes RemoveAllByOrderId (SalesActor.cs:672) — UPDATE OrderPackage SET Deleted=1 WHERE OrderID=@OrderId (OrderPackageRepository.cs:60-66), soft-deleting every package on the order; (2) SaleDetailsDrawer is opened un-hydrated (onOpenDetails={setDetailsSale}, SalesUkrainePage.tsx:958, bypassing the ensureSaleDetails hydration that openSaleSheet/openSaleDiscount use) and its save() posts {...sale} to /sales/update/file (SaleDetailsDrawer.tsx:150,191), whose handler ProcessStateUpdateSaleMessage contains the identical RemoveAllByOrderId wipe (SalesActor.cs:1083). Additionally the drawer's carrier change-history reads sale.UpdateDataCarrier (SaleDetailsDrawer.tsx:342,353), always [] for list rows, so history is permanently empty — dev DB has 56 real UpdateDataCarrier rows (sales 54429/54405/54426 carry 6-7 entries) that legacy displays (sale.details.view.tsx:742-747) and the console silently hides. Legacy is safe on the same endpoints because it posts the includeDetails=true sale whose real non-new/non-deleted packages route into RemoveAllByOrderIdExceptProvided+Update (SalesActor.cs:565-620) and survive. OrderPackages are real production data created by the legacy warehouse-Ukraine packing flow via the same /sales/update (warehouse.ukraine.sales.view.tsx:233-267) with no IsAcceptedToPacking gate on packing, so packages can exist when either console action fires. Caveat: dev DB currently has 0 OrderPackage rows, so the wipe half is code-proven (deterministic destructive write) rather than dev-data-reproduced; the empty-history half is proven against live dev data.
confirmed.fix: Console-side (server contract must stay — legacy depends on empty-packages⇒remove-all). In gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/pages/SalesUkrainePage.tsx: (1) in requestWillNotShip's onConfirm, hydrate before posting: const next = await ensureSaleDetails(sale); if (!next) throw new Error(...); await updateSale({ ...next, IsAcceptedToPacking: true }); (2) add an openSaleDetails callback mirroring openSaleSheet (const next = await ensureSaleDetails(sale); if (next) setDetailsSale(next)) and replace onOpenDetails={setDetailsSale} at line 958 with onOpenDetails={(target) => void openSaleDetails(target)}. This single hydration (via /sales/get GetByNetId, which loads both OrderPackages and UpdateDataCarrier — SaleRepository.cs:9490) simultaneously stops both package-wipe paths and makes the drawer's CarrierHistory show the real change history. Optional server hardening (separate change, all clients benefit): in both SalesActor handlers skip the package block entirely when the posted Order.OrderPackages is empty AND no package-bearing client flow requires remove-all-on-empty — but verify legacy warehouse remove-all semantics first; the console hydration fix alone is sufficient and risk-free.

confirmed.claim: ProcessUpdateOneTimeDiscountsOnSaleMessage Packaging branch calculates pricings (:1845) and snapshots totalAmount/totalAmountLocal (:1848) BEFORE persisting the new discounts (:1851-1867) and never refetches; UpdateOneTimeDiscount writes only OneTimeDiscount (OrderItemRepository.cs:156) so the delta is always 0, `increased` (:1886) is always false, and the non-increased Paid branch (:2277-2300) unconditionally flips Paid→Overpaid with overpaidAmount=0 and sets IsAcceptedToPacking=true; the returned Sale (:2722) carries pre-update CurrentPrice/EurToUah.
confirmed.expected: Refetch the sale after persisting discounts and recompute (like the New branch :1821-1823); run debt/overpaid reconciliation only on a real non-zero delta.
confirmed.explanation: CONFIRMED end-to-end. In SalesActor.cs ProcessUpdateOneTimeDiscountsOnSaleMessage's Packaging branch, the sale is fetched (:1840) and priced (:1845) BEFORE the new discounts are persisted (:1851-1867 via OrderItemRepository.UpdateOneTimeDiscount, OrderItemRepository.cs:156-162, which writes only OneTimeDiscount/DiscountUpdatedByID/OneTimeDiscountComment), and — unlike the New branch which refetches and recomputes (:1821-1827) — is never refetched. Sale.TotalAmount/OrderItem.TotalAmount are plain auto-properties set only by SaleActorsHelpers.CalculatePricingsForSaleWithDynamicPrices (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:220), so at :1886 `increased = totalAmount < sale.TotalAmount` compares a value to itself: always false, and every delta is exactly 0. Consequently, for any Packaging-stage sale with an income payment (GetLastBySaleId != null), ANY save in the discount modal (even a comment-only edit — the switch runs unconditionally) hits the non-increased branches: the Paid case (:2277-2300) writes OverpaidAmount=0 (:2290), flips payment status Paid→Overpaid (:2292) and forces IsAcceptedToPacking=true (:2293); the Overpaid case (:2301-2341) is even worse — it debits the client agreement balance by the existing OverpaidAmount (:2313-2320) and reduces or deletes the IncomePaymentOrderSale allocation (:2329-2337). The response at :2722 also returns the pre-update sale whose CurrentPrice/TotalAmountEurToUah predate the discount write (verified in dev DB: dbo.GetCalculatedProductPriceWithSharesAndVat applies OrderItem.OneTimeDiscount in its final price expression, so a refetch would differ). Reachability: legacy's modal is read-only for non-New sales (gba_client sale.discount.modal.tsx:55,153) so the branch was dormant, but the console explicitly allows editable discount modals for lifecycle Packaging=1 (gba_console saleStatus.ts:69-73 keys 0/1/2) and posts to /sales/discount/update (salesUkraineApi.ts:230-235 → SalesController.cs:1348), and Paid-at-Packaging is the normal pre-packing state. One correction to the proposed fix: a refetch alone will NOT produce a meaningful delta, because for Packaging sales TotalAmount = PricePerItem*Qty (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:158-167) and PricePerItem is frozen at packing time by SavePricesPerItem (SalesActor.cs:3508); OrderItemRepository.UpdatePricePerItem has zero callers. So the delta is structurally 0 even post-refetch, and the reconciliation must additionally be gated on a real non-zero delta to stop the corrupting writes.
confirmed.fix: Server-side, SalesActor.cs Packaging branch of ProcessUpdateOneTimeDiscountsOnSaleMessage: (1) after the persist loop (:1867), refetch and recompute exactly like the New branch — `sale = saleRepository.GetByNetId(message.Sale.NetUid); SaleActorsHelpers.CalculatePricingsForSaleWithDynamicPrices(sale, exchangeRateRepository, currencyRepository);` — so the returned Sale (:2722) and TotalAmountEurToUah reflect the persisted discounts; (2) compute the delta from the refetched totals and wrap the entire debt/payment reconciliation (:1869-2719) in `if (delta != 0)` for both directions, so zero-delta updates (today: ALL updates, since PricePerItem is frozen) become no-ops instead of flipping Paid→Overpaid (:2290-2293) or debiting balances/payment allocations (:2313-2337); (3) decision item: if Packaging-stage discount edits are meant to change the amount actually owed, the branch must also recompute and persist PricePerItem from the new dynamic CurrentPrice (the existing, currently-uncalled OrderItemRepository.UpdatePricePerItem) before recomputing totals — only then can the reconciliation delta ever be non-zero. Owning side is the server; both clients benefit and no client change is required.

confirmed.area: server single-sale EurToUah base for Packaged/Packaging sales (residual after today's fix)
confirmed.claim: Single-sale helper overwrites Product.CurrentPrice = PricePerItem for Packaged sales (:170) BEFORE computing CurrentPriceEurToUah (:174), so TotalAmountEurToUah = PricePerItem × current rate; live dev DB confirms PricePerItem EXCLUDES OneTimeDiscount (item 2036974: 57.40 vs discounted 51.66), so /sales/get (console hydration) shows the UAH figure WITHOUT the разова знижка while the list/pivot (canonical plural :481-482, overwrite after :504) shows it discounted. Same PricePerItem-based EurToUah pattern in BaseSalesGetActor.cs:977-989 (orders/items/update response — PATCH money ≠ subsequent GET) and OrderItemsActor.cs:598-620.
confirmed.expected: Compute EurToUah from dynamic OTD-inclusive Product.CurrentPrice before any PricePerItem overwrite, matching canonical plural helper (:444).
confirmed.explanation: Confirmed end-to-end with one scope correction. In SaleActorsHelpers.CalculatePricingsForSaleWithDynamicPrices (singular, /sales/get path), the else branch overwrites Product.CurrentPrice = PricePerItem at :169-172 gated on SaleLifeCycleType.Packaged BEFORE :174-176 compute CurrentPriceEurToUah/TotalAmountEurToUah, so Packaged sales return EurToUah from the undiscounted locked PricePerItem. The canonical plural helper (:481-482) and today's fixed list SQL (SaleRepository.PopulateSalesListTotals, SaleRepository.cs:162, 178-183) both compute EurToUah from the dynamic OTD-inclusive CurrentPrice for ALL lifecycle types and defer the PricePerItem overwrite (:504), so list and detail disagree for Packaged sales. Verified: dbo.GetCalculatedProductPriceWithSharesAndVat applies OrderItem.OneTimeDiscount (function body lines 282-305); SaleRepository.GetByNetId populates CurrentPrice via it (SaleRepository.cs:9519-9528); item 2036974 = 57.40 PricePerItem vs 51.66 dynamic (10% OTD); commit e9b9a402 only swapped the rate to currentExchangeRateEurToUah, leaving the Packaged base overwrite (residual confirmed via git show). Real-data impact: 209/500 (42%) of the most recent live Packaged-sale order items have dynamic price != PricePerItem (>0.005 EUR), so the console detail's UAH figure diverges from the list routinely; the 3 live OTD sales (all currently Packaging, type 1) will lose the разова знижка in the detail UAH figure once they reach Packaged. SCOPE CORRECTION: enum is New=0, Packaging=1, Packaged=2 — the singular helper's gate is Packaged only, so Packaging-stage sales (including cited item 2036974's sale 672156) render correctly in /sales/get today; the finding's 'Packaged/Packaging' title overstates. However the secondary sites are broader: BaseSalesGetActor.cs:977-989 (orders/items update response, reached via OrderItemsController.cs:184 -> UpdateOrderItemMessage -> OrderItemsActor.cs:899/1106 forward of GetOrderItemAndSaleStatisticMessage) and OrderItemsActor.cs:599-616 overwrite CurrentPrice = PricePerItem whenever PricePerItem != 0 (any billed sale, any lifecycle), then derive EurToUah — so the item-update response UAH money mismatches the subsequent /sales/get for any drifted/OTD item, including 2036974 right now.
confirmed.fix: Server-side (benefits both clients), three sites, all the same minimal reorder — compute EurToUah from the dynamic OTD-inclusive CurrentPrice before any PricePerItem overwrite: (1) src/Global.Business.Assistant.Commercial.Actors/Actors/Sales/SaleActorsHelpers.cs — in the singular helper's else branch, move lines 174-176 (CurrentPriceEurToUah = CurrentPrice * currentExchangeRateEurToUah; TotalAmountEurToUah = CurrentPriceEurToUah * Qty) ABOVE the if (SaleLifeCycleType.Packaged) block at :169-172 (or capture `decimal dynamicPrice = orderItem.Product.CurrentPrice;` at loop start and use it at :174), exactly matching the plural helper's ordering (:481-482 before :504). (2) src/Global.Business.Assistant.Commercial.Actors/Actors/Sales/SalesGetActors/BaseSalesGetActor.cs:977-989 — hoist the CurrentPriceEurToUah (:982) and TotalAmountEurToUah (:988-989) computations above the if (!orderItem.PricePerItem.Equals(decimal.Zero)) overwrite at :977-980, using the dynamic CurrentPrice returned by GetWithCalculatedProductPrices. (3) src/Global.Business.Assistant.Commercial.Actors/Actors/Sales/OrderItemsActor.cs:599-616 — same hoist: compute CurrentPriceEurToUah/TotalAmountEurToUah from the dynamic CurrentPrice before the :599-602 PricePerItem overwrite. Leave TotalAmount/TotalAmountLocal and the post-hoc CurrentPrice = PricePerItem display overwrites untouched (legacy-faithful frozen transaction price).

confirmed.claim: Sale is loaded via GetByNetId and handed directly to ConsignmentNoteDocumentManager with NO pricing recompute; the manager prints Product.CurrentLocalPrice-based unit prices and sums (ConsignmentNoteDocumentManager.cs:880,886,901,499) = today's dynamic price at today's rate, so a printed накладна for an invoiced sale drifts from the invoice whenever prices/rates changed since billing. PLAUSIBLE — verify intended behavior.
confirmed.expected: Freeze non-New prices (PricePerItem × ExchangeRateAmount) like the invoice-document paths do.
confirmed.explanation: Traced end-to-end and reproduced on live dev data. (1) BaseConsignmentNoteSettingsGetActor.cs:128-133 loads the sale via SaleRepository.GetByNetId and hands it straight to ConsignmentNoteDocumentManager.GetPrintSaleConsignmentNoteDocument — no pricing helper, no freeze block (the file contains no CalculatePricings call; ConsignmentNoteDocumentManager.cs has zero references to PricePerItem). (2) GetByNetId (SaleRepository.cs:9490, price columns at 9519-9528) fills Product.CurrentPrice/CurrentLocalPrice from dbo.GetCalculatedProductLocalPriceWithSharesAndVat; I read the live function body: it prices from TODAY'S ProductPricing + extra charge + current ProductGroupDiscount + OneTimeDiscount, and freezes only the FX rate (uses OrderItem.ExchangeRateAmount when Sale.IsPrintedPaymentInvoice=1, else current rate) — the base price is never frozen. (3) The document (worksheet "TTN" — товарно-транспортна накладна, printed via /consignment/note/settings/print/document by BOTH clients: gba_client/src/store/consignment.note.settings/epics.ts:88 and gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/api/salesUkraineApi.ts:412) prints item.Product.CurrentLocalPrice as unit price (ConsignmentNoteDocumentManager.cs:880/886), line total (901) and cargo value/VAT base (499-512). (4) Canonical semantics freeze non-New sales at PricePerItem×ExchangeRateAmount (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:157-187), and the invoice-document actor does exactly that before printing (BaseSalesGetActor.cs:246 + explicit freeze at 275-280), so an invoice and a ТТН for the same sale print different numbers whenever the price list changed after billing. (5) Live dev DB proof: of the 2000 most recent order items on non-New sales with IsPrintedPaymentInvoice=1, 745 would print with >0.05 UAH/unit drift, 593 with >1 UAH/unit, max 1576.93 UAH/unit; concrete example Sale 672156 / OrderItem 2036974: invoiced local price 57.40, a ТТН printed today shows 51.66 (~10% off). The half-frozen design (rate frozen on IsPrintedPaymentInvoice, base price live) demonstrates the freeze intent and rules out "always print current price" as intended behavior. This is a server defect shared by both clients, not a legacy-faithful quirk.
confirmed.fix: Server-side, in /root/projects/gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Commercial.Actors/Actors/ConsignmentNoteSettings/ConsignmentNoteSettingsGetActors/BaseConsignmentNoteSettingsGetActor.cs, ProcessExportConsignmentNoteDocument else-branch (after line 128): gate exactly like SaleActorsHelpers.cs:135 and mirror the invoice actor's freeze block (BaseSalesGetActor.cs:275-280) — if (!sale.BaseLifeCycleStatus.SaleLifeCycleType.Equals(SaleLifeCycleType.New) || sale.IsPaymentBillDownloaded) { foreach (OrderItem orderItem in sale.Order.OrderItems) { orderItem.Product.CurrentPrice = orderItem.PricePerItem; orderItem.Product.CurrentLocalPrice = orderItem.PricePerItem * orderItem.ExchangeRateAmount; } } before calling GetPrintSaleConsignmentNoteDocument(message.PathToFolder, sale, message.Setting). This freezes unit prices (ConsignmentNoteDocumentManager.cs:880/886), line sums (901) and the cargo-value/VAT totals (499-512), which all read Product.CurrentLocalPrice. Prefer this direct block over calling CalculatePricingsForSaleWithDynamicPrices: that helper overwrites CurrentLocalPrice only for Packaged sales (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:169-172), which would leave Shipped/Bill statuses still dynamic. Keep New un-billed sales on dynamic prices (PricePerItem is not yet meaningful there). No client changes needed — both UIs call the same endpoint.

confirmed.claim: Both grids gate the edited-invoice pencil and is-edited row class on sale.HistoryInvoiceEdit.length (SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1159/1165/1282; SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:941), but includeDetails=false ToSaleListItem hardcodes HistoryInvoiceEdit=[] (SalesController.cs:1014) and the projected Sale.IsEdited is never populated by any list SQL (SaleRepository.cs:2452-2816) — indicators only flash after per-row hydration and vanish on the next reload; legacy shows them on every row via includeDetails=true (sale.item.tsx:353-415).
confirmed.expected: Compute a real IsEdited/history-count in the list projection server-side and gate the console indicator on it.
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. The console sales grids load via /sales/all/filtered with includeDetails=false (gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/api/salesUkraineApi.ts:32, sales-online-shop/api/salesOnlineShopApi.ts:11). On that path the server projection ToSaleListItem hardcodes HistoryInvoiceEdit = Array.Empty<object>() (gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Api/Controllers/Sales/SalesController.cs:1014) and serializes sale.IsEdited (line 773) which is EF-ignored (SaleMap.cs:93) and never assigned anywhere in the server (zero `IsEdited =` assignments; SaleRepository.cs has 0 IsEdited references), so it is always false. In SaleRepository.GetAllRangedByLifeCycleType the !includeDetails branch returns at line 2853-2856, before the HistoryInvoiceEdit load at lines 3200-3241 which only runs for includeDetails=true. Therefore the console gates at SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1159 (is-edited row class, applied 1165) and 1282 (Рахунок редаговано pencil) and SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:941 can never fire from list data; they appear only after per-row hydration (ensureSaleDetails -> getSaleById -> replaceSaleInList, SalesUkrainePage.tsx:336-363; GetByNetId does load history at SaleRepository.cs:10195-10205) and vanish on the next full/background reload (setSales(next) at line 538; realtime reload at 449-455). Blast radius extends to warehouse-ukraine SalesTab (api/salesApi.ts:24,28 uses includeDetails=false + fromShipments=true), whose gate at SalesTab.tsx:621 (HistoryInvoiceEdit?.length && !IsPrinted) is equally dead — legacy warehouse relies on exactly that signal to flag edited-but-not-reprinted invoices (gba_client warehouse.ukraine.sales.view.tsx:400,445-458). Legacy sales pivot gets the indicators on every row because it calls /sales/all/filtered without includeDetails (defaults true, SalesController.cs:702; api.constants.ts:612) and renders them in sale.item.tsx:345-415. Dev DB has only one (deleted) sale with HistoryInvoiceEdit rows, but the defect is structural: whenever the invoice-edit flow creates history rows, the console list cannot show the edited state — operationally risky since the flag exists to prevent shipping/printing stale invoices.
confirmed.fix: Server owns the fix (both console pages + warehouse tab benefit; legacy unaffected since it uses includeDetails=true and ignores IsEdited). 1) gba-server SaleRepository.PopulateSalesListTotals (Global.Business.Assistant.Operations.Infrastructure.Persistence/Sales/Repositories/SaleRepository.cs:141, already invoked on the !includeDetails path at line 2854 for both normal and fromShipments lists): add one aggregate query and set the existing ignored flag — foreach (long saleId in _connection.Query<long>("SELECT [SaleID] FROM [HistoryInvoiceEdit] WHERE [Deleted] = 0 AND [SaleID] IN " + IdsJsonSql + " GROUP BY [SaleID]", new { IdsJson = salesIdsJson })) { if (salesById.TryGetValue(saleId, out Sale s)) s.IsEdited = true; } — IsEdited already exists on Sale (Sale.cs:155) and is already serialized by ToSaleListItem (SalesController.cs:773), so the list payload changes without any contract addition. 2) gba_console: add IsEdited?: boolean to SalesUkraineSale (features/sales-ukraine/types.ts) and the online-shop sale type (features/sales-online-shop/types.ts) and warehouse type (features/warehouse-ukraine/types.ts). 3) Gate on the union of flag and hydrated rows: SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1159 -> const isEdited = Boolean(sale.IsEdited) || (Array.isArray(sale.HistoryInvoiceEdit) && sale.HistoryInvoiceEdit.length > 0); reuse isEdited at line 1282 instead of the inline HistoryInvoiceEdit check; SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:941 -> same combined predicate; warehouse-ukraine SalesTab.tsx:621 -> return (Boolean(sale.IsEdited) || Boolean(sale.HistoryInvoiceEdit?.length)) && !sale.IsPrinted.

confirmed.area: console positions column falls back to unit quantity
confirmed.claim: getOrderItemCount = OrderItems?.length || Order?.TotalCount || sale.TotalCount with NO TotalPositions; the list payload always has OrderItems=[] (SalesController.cs:938) so every collapsed row shows Order.TotalCount = SUM(Qty) in float UNITS (SaleRepository.cs:154/219) labeled «поз.» (3 lines / 40 units → «40 поз.»), flipping to the real line count after hydration; TotalPositions is also missing from SalesOnlineShopSale (sales-online-shop/types.ts:130-164). The fixed sales-ukraine variant (SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1881) keeps the same Order.TotalCount||TotalCount tail fallbacks after TotalPositions — latent identical confusion also misgating canExpand/showEditShift/discountEditable.
confirmed.expected: Use Sale.TotalPositions (line COUNT, present in the list payload, SaleRepository.cs:155/212); fallback chain must stop at OrderItems.length || TotalPositions, never TotalCount.
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. The console online-shop list fetches /sales/all/filtered with includeDetails=false (salesOnlineShopApi.ts:5-11); the server list payload (SalesController.cs ToSaleListItem) always has Order.OrderItems=[] (line 938), HasDetails=false (778), and carries BOTH sale.TotalCount=SUM(Qty) float units and sale.TotalPositions=COUNT of lines (lines 769-770; computed in SaleRepository.cs PopulateSalesListTotals, lines 158-159/252-253, invoked at 2853-2854). getOrderItemCount (SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:1532-1534) omits TotalPositions and falls back OrderItems.length -> Order.TotalCount -> TotalCount, so every collapsed row renders the unit sum under the «поз.» label (lines 968-969). Live dev DB confirms real impact: sale 672155 = 3 lines / 27 units, so the row shows «27 поз.» and flips to «3 поз.» after expand/open when ensureSaleDetails (lines 226-254) swaps in the hydrated sale with populated OrderItems. Additional hard misgating on real data: sale 662156 has 1 line with SUM(Qty)=0.0, so getOrderItemCount=0 makes canExpand (line 649) and discountEditable (line 827) false — that sale can never be expanded and its discount never edited despite having a position. SalesOnlineShopSale (types.ts:130-164) also lacks TotalPositions. The sales-ukraine variant (SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1881) already has TotalPositions second in the chain; its trailing TotalCount fallbacks are latent-only (both aggregates come from the same list query, so TotalPositions=0 implies TotalCount=0) — not an active defect there.
confirmed.fix: Console owns the fix (server already exposes TotalPositions in the list payload; no server change needed). In /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-online-shop/pages/SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:1533 change getOrderItemCount to: return sale.Order?.OrderItems?.length || getNumber(sale.TotalPositions) || 0 — dropping the Order.TotalCount/TotalCount unit-sum fallbacks entirely. Add TotalPositions?: number to SalesOnlineShopSale in /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-online-shop/types.ts (the ~line 157 block). Optionally apply the same tail-trim in /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/pages/SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1881 (remove getNumber(sale.Order?.TotalCount) || getNumber(sale.TotalCount)) to kill the latent identical confusion.

confirmed.claim: Discount badge derives uniform/average OneTimeDiscount + DiscountUpdatedBy from sale.Order.OrderItems, but the list endpoint always sends OrderItems=[] (SalesController.cs:938); the badge appears only after hydration and disappears after every reload() — including immediately after saving a discount (onSaved → reload, :1000-1003), while the legacy pivot shows it on every row (sale.item.tsx:277-295).
confirmed.expected: Add a sale-level discount field to ToSaleListItem/list SQL (or hydrate) so the badge renders on un-expanded rows.
confirmed.explanation: Console list rows can never show the Знижка badge: getSalesUkraine requests /sales/all/filtered with includeDetails=false (salesUkraineApi.ts:25-32), and the server's ToSaleListItem projection hard-codes Order.OrderItems = Array.Empty<object>() (SalesController.cs:938; HasDetails=false at :778) — the list SQL (SaleRepository.PopulateSalesListTotals:141-263) never even loads OrderItems, so no discount data exists in the payload. The badge derivation at SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1154-1158 reads sale.Order.OrderItems, and both getUniformOneTimeDiscount and getAverageOneTimeDiscount return null on empty arrays (saleDiscounts.ts:16-17,30-31), so collapsed rows render only the generic percent icon. The badge appears only after ensureSaleDetails hydration via /sales/get (SalesUkrainePage.tsx:336-363), and every reload() fully replaces the list (loadSales:525-561, setSales:538), wiping hydration — including immediately after saving a discount (SaleDiscountModal.tsx:113-115 → onSaved → reload at SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1000-1003), so the just-saved badge vanishes; realtime background reloads (:448-458) wipe it too. Legacy always shows it: gba_client never passes includeDetails (server default true, SalesController.cs:702) and renders OneTimeDiscount % + DiscountUpdatedBy.LastName per row (sale.item.tsx:277-295). Live dev DB confirms real discounted sales exist (e.g. Sale 672156, 2026-07-03, OneTimeDiscount=10). This is dead UI on the primary payload plus misleading disappear-after-save behavior, not a cosmetic or intentional difference.
confirmed.fix: Server-owned fix (benefits any includeDetails=false consumer). 1) In SaleRepository.PopulateSalesListTotals (gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Operations.Infrastructure.Persistence/Sales/Repositories/SaleRepository.cs:146-222) extend the aggregate SELECT with discount summary columns from [OrderItem].OneTimeDiscount: MIN, MAX, AVG, SUM(CASE WHEN OneTimeDiscount > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DiscountedPositions, and the first item's updater last name (e.g. correlated TOP(1) [User].LastName ordered by OrderItem.ID joined on DiscountUpdatedByID). 2) Add EF-ignored sale-level properties alongside TotalPositions (e.g. Sale.OneTimeDiscountUniform, Sale.OneTimeDiscountAverage, Sale.DiscountUpdatedByLastName), populated in the totals loop (:228-262) with legacy rules: uniform = MIN when MIN=MAX>0; average = AVG when DiscountedPositions = TotalPositions; else null. 3) Emit these scalars in ToSaleListItem (SalesController.cs projection, sale-level block near :778). 4) Console: in SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1154-1158 fall back to the sale-level fields when rowOrderItems is empty (keep item-derived values for hydrated rows so the modal-edited state stays live).

confirmed.claim: The red partial-sale icon is gated on sale.MisplacedSaleId (:890,:1135), but ToSaleListItem omits MisplacedSaleId from the list projection (SalesController.cs:721-1016; field exists on Sale.cs:79 and in legacy includeDetails=true payloads) — the badge never renders on un-expanded rows.
confirmed.expected: Serialize MisplacedSaleId in the list projection so the badge matches legacy (sale.item.client.name.tsx:31-35).
confirmed.explanation: Confirmed end-to-end. The console online-shop list fetches /sales/all/filtered with includeDetails=false (gba_console/src/features/sales-online-shop/api/salesOnlineShopApi.ts:11), which makes the server return the ToSaleListItem projection (gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Api/Controllers/Sales/SalesController.cs:714, method at :721-1016). That anonymous projection serializes ~45 Sale fields (including RetailClientId) but omits MisplacedSaleId, so sale.MisplacedSaleId is undefined on every list row and the red «Часткова продажа» badge gated on it (SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:890) can never render on un-expanded rows. Legacy sends no includeDetails param (server default true → full Sale entities; list SQL selects [Sale].* at SaleRepository.cs:2552; property at Sale.cs:79 with no ignore attribute) and renders the badge directly from the list payload (gba_client sale.item.tsx:168 → sale.item.client.name.tsx:72-85, linking to the incomplete-sale page). The feature is live: OrderService.cs:804-820/1068-1077 sets Sale.MisplacedSaleId when an ecommerce order has misplaced items (18 MisplacedSale rows in dev DB, latest 2026-06-03; the id is cleared on resolution, so pending partial sales exist transiently — exactly when the indicator matters). The only way the console badge appears is after the user expands/opens that row, because ensureSaleDetails (SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:227-255) hydrates via /sales/get (GetByNetId selects [Sale].*, SaleRepository.cs:9494) and replaces the row — confirming the badge is dead on the initial list render, a real dead-field regression versus legacy, not a cosmetic difference.
confirmed.fix: Server-side, one line: add `sale.MisplacedSaleId,` to the anonymous object in ToSaleListItem (gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Api/Controllers/Sales/SalesController.cs, inside the projection starting at :721 — e.g. next to sale.RetailClientId around :739). The server should own the fix because the light projection is the shared includeDetails=false contract and the console types already declare the field (gba_console/src/features/sales-online-shop/types.ts:148); no console change is needed (truthiness gating at SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:890/:1135 works as soon as the field arrives; the number-vs-string TS type mismatch mirrors legacy and has no runtime effect).

confirmed.claim: Three surfaces show item.PricePerItem (EUR, frozen at item-add, discount-blind, 0/stale for New dynamic-price sales) unlabeled next to Сума = TotalAmountLocal labeled agreement currency: SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1711 detail sheet, SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:1368 detail drawer, SaleEditorDrawer.tsx:641-642 (also copySaleData :297). confirmed.expected: Show a unit price consistent with the row total (TotalAmountLocal/Qty or Product.CurrentPrice/CurrentLocalPrice for New sales), or explicitly label the column EUR and pair with TotalAmount.
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. All three console surfaces render raw OrderItem.PricePerItem (EUR, frozen at item-add, discount-blind) under an unlabeled «Ціна» column directly beside a «Сума» column that shows a local/agreement-currency total with an explicit currency label: SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1711 (Сума = TotalAmountLocal ?? TotalAmount labeled agreement code at :1712-1714), SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:1368 (same at :1350/:1370), SaleEditorDrawer.tsx:641-642 (Сума at :665-666 = TotalAmountEurToUah for non-VAT EUR sales, else TotalAmountLocal), plus copySaleData at SaleEditorDrawer.tsx:297. All are hydrated via /sales/get (GetSaleByNetIdActor.cs:40 → SaleActorsHelpers.CalculatePricingsForSaleWithDynamicPrices). Server math confirms the mismatch: for non-New sales TotalAmountLocal = PricePerItem × Qty × ExchangeRateAmount (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:158-167), and for New non-bill-downloaded sales totals are recomputed from dynamic Product.CurrentPrice/CurrentLocalPrice while PricePerItem is never updated (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:135-148), so разова знижка/extra-charge/price changes make Ціна × К-сть ≠ Сума even for EUR agreements. Live dev DB ground truth: UAH-agreement invoice items carry EUR PricePerItem with ExchangeRateAmount = 51.5 (e.g. 1.1099 EUR unit price rendering beside a 57.16 UAH row total), and this is the dominant data shape — 100,351 UAH invoice items vs 136 EUR; 3 of 51 New-sale items have PricePerItem = 0 (Ціна shows 0.00 beside a nonzero Сума). Legacy never does this: expanded sale rows show no unit price at all, only currency-labeled totals + Qty (gba_client sale.expand.content.item.tsx:75-113), and the legacy editor shows server-normalized Product.CurrentPrice explicitly labeled 'Euro' plus a matching local unit price labeled with the currency code (ShoppingCartGrid.tsx:58-69,116-117; server sets Product.CurrentPrice = PricePerItem for non-New sales at SaleActorsHelpers.cs:170/185, dynamic for New). This is a real data defect (wrong currency next to labeled local total; stale/zero/discount-blind price for New sales), not a cosmetic difference. Only the copySaleData path is internally currency-consistent (pairs PricePerItem with EUR TotalAmount first) but still exports the stale/zero price for New dynamic-price sales.
confirmed.fix: Console-side fix (server already emits correct, total-consistent fields on every /sales/get). Derive the unit price from the same value the Сума column displays, so it is correct by construction in every sale state and currency: (1) gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/pages/SalesUkrainePage.tsx SaleDetailProductRow (~:1693,1711): const qty = getNumber(item.Qty); render formatAmount(amount != null && qty ? amount / qty : null) instead of item.PricePerItem (amount is already computed on :1693). (2) Same change in gba_console/src/features/sales-online-shop/pages/SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx (~:1350,1368). (3) gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/components/SaleEditorDrawer.tsx useItemColumns 'price' column (:641-642): compute the same per-row total used by the 'total' column (useEurToUah ? TotalAmountEurToUah : TotalAmountLocal ?? TotalAmount) divided by Qty (guard Qty 0/null); alternatively mirror legacy exactly by showing item.Product.CurrentPrice with an explicit 'EUR' header plus a local unit-price column — but the divide-by-Qty variant is the minimal change. (4) copySaleData (:297): replace getNumber(item.PricePerItem) with getNumber(item.Product?.CurrentPrice) (server-normalized: = PricePerItem for non-New, = dynamic effective price for New) or TotalAmount/Qty, keeping the exported Ціна consistent with the exported EUR Сума for New dynamic-price sales; add CurrentPrice to the OrderItem Product type in the feature types.ts (field is present in the /sales/get payload — legacy consumes it — just not declared in console types).

confirmed.area: console retail client card sale items
confirmed.claim: getRetailItemUnitPrice prefers Product.CurrentLocalPrice (today's dynamic price) while the row total is the sale's historical TotalAmountLocal (PricePerItem × ExchangeRateAmount for non-New sales, BaseRetailClientGetActor.cs:203-233), so unit×qty ≠ total whenever the price changed since the sale; the fallback then drops to PricePerItem (EUR) beside a UAH total (rendered OnlineShopOrderItemsList.tsx:50,73-75).

confirmed.explanation: Confirmed end-to-end. /retail/clients/sales (BaseRetailClientGetActor.ProcessGetAllSalesByRetailClientNetIdMessage, gba-server .../RetailClientGetActors/BaseRetailClientGetActor.cs:123-147) returns sales of ALL lifecycle statuses with includeDetails=true; SaleRepository.cs:2897-2906 populates Product.CurrentLocalPrice via dbo.GetCalculatedProductLocalPriceWithSharesAndVat = TODAY's dynamic price. In the actor's non-New branch (BaseRetailClientGetActor.cs:202-234) TotalAmountLocal = PricePerItem × Qty × ExchangeRateAmount (historical, sale-time rate); Product.CurrentPrice is overwritten to historical PricePerItem (line 231) but Product.CurrentLocalPrice is only rounded and stays dynamic (232-233). Console getRetailItemUnitPrice (gba_console/src/features/clients/onlineShopDisplay.ts:17-18) prefers Product.CurrentLocalPrice while getRetailItemTotal (line 4) resolves to TotalAmountLocal; both are rendered per row in OnlineShopOrderItemsList.tsx:73-80 fed by sale.Order.OrderItems (OnlineShopSalesPanel.tsx:140-143). So for every non-New retail sale, displayed unit × qty ≠ displayed total whenever the price/rate drifted. Live dev DB proof on the identical non-New code path: sale 662147 row total 903.19 UAH vs today's unit 901.44 (×1); sale 662148 row total 384.28 vs unit×qty 382.95; gap unbounded as rates move off the frozen ExchangeRateAmount (51.5). Not legacy-faithful: legacy's equivalent screen (fast.client.all.sales.tsx → sale.expand.content.item.tsx:75-113) shows no unit price at all on sale rows; legacy uses CurrentLocalPrice only in the live-cart view (fast.clients.online.shop.tsx:95) where the total is also derived from it, so it stays internally consistent. The EUR-PricePerItem-beside-UAH-total fallback in the claim only fires when Product is absent (cart-JSON shapes), not for sale OrderItems. confirmed.fix: Console-side fix (the unit-price display is a console invention; legacy never reads CurrentLocalPrice on sale rows, so a server change would risk legacy payload consumers for no benefit). In gba_console/src/features/clients/onlineShopDisplay.ts, make getRetailItemUnitPrice derive the unit price from the same source as the row total when a stored total exists: unit = (Total ?? Sum ?? TotalAmountLocal ?? TotalAmount) / qty when qty > 0 and a total field is present; only fall back to Product.CurrentLocalPrice ?? UnitPrice ?? PricePerItem ?? Price when no stored total exists (live ShoppingCartJson cart items). This keeps cart behavior identical (cart items lack TotalAmountLocal), is exact for New sales (server computes TotalAmountLocal from CurrentLocalPrice there), and makes unit × qty ≡ total for non-New sales. Optionally, for full server-side consistency, BaseRetailClientGetActor.CalculatePricingsForSalesWithDynamicPrices could also set orderItem.Product.CurrentLocalPrice = PricePerItem * ExchangeRateAmount in the non-New branch (mirroring line 231's CurrentPrice overwrite), but that changes a payload legacy also receives (SUCCESS_GET_SALES_BY_USERS reuses it), so the console-only derivation is the safer minimal fix.

confirmed.claim: isDiscountEditableSaleLifecycle allows lifecycles 0/1/2 (used at SaleDiscountModal.tsx:51 and SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1153/1324-1329): Packaged sales get an editable form whose save always fails (server /sales/discount/update accepts New+Packaging only, SalesActor.cs:1783/~2725 UPDATE_DISCOUNT_NOT_ALLOWED_AFTER_PACKING); Packaging sales get an edit path legacy never exposed (legacy modal read-only unless New, sale.discount.modal.tsx:55; add-percent button hidden when IsInvoice && !HasDiscount, sale.item.tsx:296-304). confirmed.expected: Restrict the add affordance to New (0), keep the form read-only for Packaged, and make the average badge non-clickable like legacy.
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. Console isDiscountEditableSaleLifecycle (gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/saleStatus.ts:69-73) returns true for lifecycles 0/1/2 and drives three surfaces: SaleDiscountModal.tsx:51 (form editable unless read-only), SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1153+1313-1329 (clickable badge/add-percent icon), SaleExpandContent.tsx:28 (per-item edit anchor). Server ground truth (gba-server SalesActor.cs ProcessUpdateOneTimeDiscountsOnSaleMessage): switch at line 1801 processes only New (1802) and Packaging (1832); Packaged/Shipping/Received/Await/default (2725-2731) return UPDATE_DISCOUNT_NOT_ALLOWED_AFTER_PACKING, which SalesController.cs:1350-1352 turns into 400 → console shows generic 'Не вдалося зберегти знижку'. Worse: SalesActor.cs:1797-1799 persists Sale.OneTimeDiscountComment (SaleRepository.cs:11946-11952 UPDATE [Sale] SET OneTimeDiscountComment...) BEFORE the lifecycle check, so a 'rejected' Packaged save still silently mutates the DB comment. Legacy ground truth: modal read-only unless New (gba_client sale.discount.modal.tsx:55, save/cancel hidden at 153); pivot add-percent button hidden when (IsInvoice && !HasDiscount) || !IsNewOrPackagingStatus (sale.item.tsx:296-304) with IsNewOrPackagingStatus = New|Packaging and IsInvoice = Packaging (sale.expand.item.tsx:52-60) → add affordance effectively New-only; mixed-discount average badge explicitly non-clickable ('not__button', no onClick, sale.item.tsx:288-294); per-item rows use the same predicate (sale.expand.content.item.tsx:116-135). So the console (a) gives Packaged sales an editable form whose save always fails AND leaks a comment write, (b) gives Packaging sales an edit path legacy never exposed in this UI, and (c) makes the average badge a clickable sale-level editor whose save (SaleDiscountModal.tsx:97-110) overwrites all differing per-item OneTimeDiscount values with one uniform value — an operation legacy deliberately blocks. One correction to the claim: legacy's UNIFORM badge is clickable at any lifecycle (opens read-only modal), so badge clickability itself is legacy-faithful; the defect is limited to lifecycle set 2, editable-at-1, and the average-badge case.
confirmed.fix: Console owns the fix (server behavior is the legacy contract). (1) gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/saleStatus.ts:72 — change isDiscountEditableSaleLifecycle to return key === '0' only (matches legacy edit gate; also fixes SaleDiscountModal read-only predicate and SaleExpandContent per-item add anchor, and makes the modal's existing 'лише для нового продажу' alert truthful). (2) gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/pages/SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1316 — gate badge clickability on uniformDiscount != null in addition to discountEditable (i.e. `{discountEditable && uniformDiscount != null ? <button ...` ) so the average badge renders the existing static span and can never trigger a uniform overwrite; line 1153 keeps discountEditable = isNewOrPackagingStatus(sale) && positions > 0, which after (1) means New only. Optional legacy-faithful extra: allow the uniform badge to stay clickable at any lifecycle since the modal self-gates read-only, but the minimal safe change is the above. Secondary server hardening (recommended, benefits all clients): in gba-server SalesActor.cs move the UpdateDiscountComment call at lines 1797-1799 inside the New/Packaging branches so a rejected Packaged update no longer persists the sale-level OneTimeDiscountComment before returning UPDATE_DISCOUNT_NOT_ALLOWED_AFTER_PACKING.

confirmed.claim: Header money pair shows localRetailPrice = pricing?.RetailPriceLocal ?? product.CurrentPriceEurToUah under label "UAH" beside the client's EUR sale price; RetailPriceLocal is the RETAIL «ЦР» price × rate (GetProductWithPricesAndDiscountsActor.cs:95-119) and is a non-nullable decimal so the client-price fallback essentially never fires — the manager reads retail-UAH as the client's UAH price, diverging for any discounted/wholesale pricing.
confirmed.expected: The UAH companion of the client's EUR price should be product.CurrentPriceEurToUah (legacy data.item.mini.details.tsx:159-164); retail belongs only in the separate «Роздріб» block.
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. ProductFullDetailPanel.tsx:82 sets localRetailPrice = pricing?.RetailPriceLocal ?? product.CurrentPriceEurToUah and line 133 renders it under a bare "UAH" label immediately beside the "EUR" metric holding salePrice = pricing?.DiscountPriceEUR (the client's discounted agreement price, line 80). The pricing object comes from /products/pricings/all (newSaleWizardApi.ts:310-316 → ProductsController.cs:1027-1032 → GetProductWithPricesAndDiscountsActor), where RetailPriceLocal = euroExchangeRate.Amount * RetailPriceEUR in all three branches (GetProductWithPricesAndDiscountsActor.cs:95-97/106-108/117-119) and RetailPriceEUR is the «ЦP» retail pricing (lines 41, 62-69) — NOT the client's price. RetailPriceLocal is a non-nullable decimal (CalculatedPricingsWithDiscounts.cs:10) always assigned, so the CurrentPriceEurToUah fallback never fires when pricing loads. Legacy semantics: the UAH companion of the client price is CurrentPriceEurToUah (gba_client data.item.mini.details.tsx:159-164; server GetSingleProductRepository.cs:216 computes it as CurrentPrice x current EUR→UAH rate), while RetailPriceLocal appears only inside an explicitly titled «Retail» block (data.item.full.details.item.tsx:155-170). The console wizard itself uses CurrentPriceEurToUah everywhere else (NewSaleProductsStep.tsx:2010/2452/2472, EditShoppingCartOverlay.tsx:55/70), so the same product shows two different UAH figures in the carousel row vs the detail header. Failure scenario: client on a wholesale tier or with a product-group discount (actor's DiscountRate branch lines 83-93 guarantees DiscountPriceEUR < RetailPriceEUR): header reads e.g. EUR 80.00 | UAH 6435 where 6435 is retail 125 EUR x 51.48 rather than 80 x 51.48 ≈ 4118 — the manager reads/quotes the retail UAH as the client's UAH price. Display-only (no persisted data corrupted), hence medium.
confirmed.fix: Console-side fix (server payload is correct; it legitimately exposes retail values for the «Роздріб» block — the console wired the wrong field into the header slot). In /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/components/new-sale-wizard/ProductFullDetailPanel.tsx: (1) line 82 — replace with a client-price source: const localSalePrice = getWizardProductNumber(product.CurrentPriceEurToUah); (2) line 133 — render localSalePrice under the "UAH" label. Optionally, for full legacy full-details parity, also show pricing?.RetailPriceLocal inside the existing «Роздріб» block (lines 152-157) next to RetailPriceEUR, where it is explicitly labeled as retail. No server change needed.

confirmed.area: console wizard product detail panel price chain
confirmed.claim: basePrice = pricing?.PriceEUR ?? CurrentPrice, but server CalculatedPricingsWithDiscounts.PriceEUR is a NON-nullable decimal left 0 when there is no product-group discount (GetProductWithPricesAndDiscountsActor.cs:99-119), so `??` never falls back and a muted «0,00 EUR» renders as the pre-discount price for most products (:147-149); same class for retailPrice = pricing?.RetailPriceEUR (:81,:152-157) showing «Роздріб 0,00 EUR» when retail pricing is absent.
confirmed.expected: Treat 0 PriceEUR/RetailPriceEUR as not-set (|| null), falling back to CurrentPrice / hiding the block.
confirmed.explanation: Confirmed end-to-end. Server: GetProductWithPricesAndDiscountsActor.cs assigns PriceEUR only in the product-group-discount branch (:87-91); the two no-discount branches (:99-104, :111-115) never set it, and CalculatedPricingsWithDiscounts.PriceEUR is a non-nullable decimal (CalculatedPricingsWithDiscounts.cs:12). GbaJsonOptions (System.Text.Json, no DefaultIgnoreCondition) serializes it as "PriceEUR": 0, so the console's `pricing?.PriceEUR ?? CurrentPrice` (ProductFullDetailPanel.tsx:79) never falls back. Since salePrice = DiscountPriceEUR = round(CurrentPrice,2) is always set, the guard `basePrice !== salePrice` (:147) passes and a muted «0,00 EUR» renders as the pre-discount price. Live dev DB: 1,087 of 4,787 active ClientAgreements have zero ProductGroupDiscount rows, so for ~23% of agreements EVERY product detail panel in the wizard shows the bogus 0,00 base price (the other 3,700 agreements carry a full 108-group active matrix and hit the discount branch; zero-rate rows there correctly yield PriceEUR==DiscountPriceEUR and hide the muted price — so the finding's "most products" is an overstatement, but the defect is real). Retail sub-claim also confirmed: RetailPriceEUR = round(CalculatePriceForPricing(...),2) where «ЦP» roots to «ЦО2» (Pricing 849); 15,641 ProductPricing rows for 849 have Price=0 and ~4,684 priced products lack an 849 row, so ~20k products (~7%) return RetailPriceEUR=0 and render «Роздріб 0,00 EUR» (:81,:152-157) plus a «UAH 0,00» header metric via RetailPriceLocal=0 (:82,:133) instead of hiding/falling back. Legacy is no baseline: it stores this payload in redux (product.reducer.ts:187) but no legacy component/template ever reads PriceEUR/RetailPriceEUR/RetailPriceLocal, so this console-only UI misinterprets server data with no legacy-faithful justification. Display-only impact (the transactional price is DiscountPriceEUR/CurrentPrice, which is correct), hence medium.
confirmed.fix: Two-sided minimal fix. Server (owns the root cause — a meaningless 0 for "not set"): in /root/projects/gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Inventory.Actors/Actors/Products/ProductsGetActors/GetProductWithPricesAndDiscountsActor.cs add `PriceEUR = Math.Round(product.CurrentPrice, 2)` to both no-discount initializers (the blocks at :99-104 and :111-115), matching the discount-branch semantics (no discount => base price equals current price); this makes basePrice===salePrice so the console's muted price correctly disappears. Console (owns the retail-0 data case, which the non-nullable DTO cannot express as absent): in /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/components/new-sale-wizard/ProductFullDetailPanel.tsx treat 0 as not-set — line 79: `const basePrice = pricing?.PriceEUR || getWizardProductNumber(product.CurrentPrice)`; line 81: `const retailPrice = pricing?.RetailPriceEUR || null`; line 82: `const localRetailPrice = pricing?.RetailPriceLocal || getWizardProductNumber(product.CurrentPriceEurToUah)`. Legacy needs no change (it never reads these fields).

confirmed.fix: Console-side only (server data is correct; component is console-only). In /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/warehouse-ukraine/components/ShipmentsTab.tsx change the add-sales column (lines 2189-2197): header from t('Вся сума') to t('Сума в EUR'), accessor to (sale) => sale.TotalAmount, cell to displayValue(sale.TotalAmount).
