confirmed.claim: getTotalRows reads TotalRowsQty only from sales[0]; ensureSaleDetails replaces a row in place with the /sales/get payload (:244) which never carries TotalRowsQty (list ROW_NUMBER SQL only), so expanding/opening the FIRST row collapses totalRows to page size and the paginator drops to 1 page until the next reload.
confirmed.expected: Read TotalRowsQty from any row that has it, or cache it at load time.
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. The list endpoint /sales/all/filtered (includeDetails=false) returns TotalRowsQty on every row (SaleRepository.cs:2773-2775 sets it from COUNT_BIG(*) OVER(); SalesController.cs:774 serializes it) plus HasDetails=false (:778). Opening/expanding ANY row triggers ensureSaleDetails (SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:226-254, gated by needsSaleDetails :1536 on HasDetails===false), which fetches /sales/get -> GetSaleByNetIdActor.cs:34-56 -> SaleRepository.GetByNetId (:9490). That single-sale query never populates TotalRowsQty (0 grep hits in the method; SaleMap.cs:95 ignores the column so Dapper leaves the default 0, and SaleActorsHelpers pricing code never sets it), and replaceSaleInList (:1540-1542) swaps the row wholesale with no field merge. When the replaced row is the FIRST row, getTotalRows (:1376-1378) reads sales[0].TotalRowsQty -> getNumber(0)=0 -> falsy -> falls back to sales.length (page size), so totalPages (:212) collapses to 1 and the shared Paginator disables the next-page chevron (Paginator.tsx:49). Clicking or expanding the first grid row is a routine interaction, and the breakage persists until a refetch (reload/filter/page-size change). The identical defect exists in SalesUkrainePage.tsx (getTotalRows :1719-1721 reads sales[0]; in-place replacement at :353).
confirmed.fix: Console-side fix (the server list payload is already correct on every row; adding TotalRowsQty to the single-sale /sales/get would be semantically wrong). Two equivalent minimal options; apply to BOTH pages: (1) In /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-online-shop/pages/SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:1376-1378 and /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/pages/SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1719-1721, change getTotalRows to scan for the first row with a truthy TotalRowsQty instead of only sales[0]: `for (const sale of sales) { const total = getNumber(sale.TotalRowsQty); if (total) return total } return sales.length`. Works because only hydrated rows lose the field. (2) Alternatively/additionally, preserve the field across hydration: in ensureSaleDetails (SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:243-244 and SalesUkrainePage.tsx:353) replace with `{ ...next, TotalRowsQty: sale.TotalRowsQty }` before calling replaceSaleInList. Option 1 is the smallest safe change; option 2 also protects any future reader of TotalRowsQty on hydrated rows.

confirmed.area: console list reload vs expanded rows
confirmed.claim: reload() replaces hydrated sales with detail-less projections (setSales(next) :538) while expandedKeys stays populated (:965-971) — every expanded panel silently empties (Order.OrderItems=[]) ~800 ms after any realtime sale event (scheduleRealtimeReload :448-458) or after any action calling reload().
confirmed.expected: Re-hydrate sales whose keys are in expandedKeys (or merge details from previous state) after reload.
confirmed.explanation: Confirmed end-to-end. Expanding a row hydrates the sale via /sales/get (SalesUkrainePage.tsx:336-363, replaceSaleInList :353). Every reload() re-runs loadSales (:525-556) which fetches /sales/all/filtered with includeDetails:false (salesUkraineApi.ts:32); the server returns the ToSaleListItem projection with NO Order property and HasDetails=false (gba-server SalesController.cs:714, 721-778). setSales(next) (:538) wholesale-replaces the list while expandedKeys is never reconciled (only mutated in toggleSaleExpand :387-414). On re-render the row key (NetUid, :940) is stable so isExpanded stays true (:942), canExpand stays true via the TotalPositions fallback in getOrderItemCount (:1881), and SaleExpandContent receives the detail-less sale (:965-971), computes orderItems=[] (SaleExpandContent.tsx:26) and renders the 'Товарів не знайдено' empty state (:33-40) — wrong data for a sale that has items. No effect re-hydrates; background realtime reloads (scheduleRealtimeReload :448-458, fired by org-wide saleAdded/saleUpdated :469-496) skip the loading skeleton (:526-532) so the panel empties silently ~800ms after any other user's sale event. Deterministic repro without realtime: expand row -> edit item discount -> save -> SaleDiscountModal.onSaved calls reload() (:1000-1003) -> expanded panel shows 'Товарів не знайдено'. Same via SaleDetailsDrawer/SaleEditDrawer/audit-confirm/NewSaleWizard onClose, which all call reload(). Legacy is unaffected (loads the pivot with includeDetails=true), so this is a console-only regression, not a legacy-faithful quirk.
confirmed.fix: Console-side fix in /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/pages/SalesUkrainePage.tsx (server projection is intentional; legacy path unaffected). Keep an expandedKeysRef (mirroring expandedKeys, like salesRef) so loadSales deps stay unchanged. In loadSales' success path replace the bare setSales(next) (:538) with merge-then-rehydrate: (1) merge — for each sale in next whose key (String(sale.NetUid||sale.Id)) is in expandedKeysRef.current and needsSaleDetails(sale), find the prior sale in salesRef.current via isSameSale and, if it was hydrated, carry over its Order and HasDetails:true onto the fresh projection (fresh header totals kept, item rows preserved, no blank flash); (2) rehydrate — for each such expanded sale fire void getSaleById(sale.NetUid).then(fresh => fresh && setSales(cur => replaceSaleInList(cur, fresh))) so the expanded item rows also reflect the change that triggered the reload (guard with the existing cancelled flag). Optionally simpler but flash-prone: skip the merge and only do step 2.

confirmed.area: server /sales/all/client (client-card sales tab) USD plural variant
confirmed.claim: CalculatePricingsForSalesWithDynamicPricesWithUsdCalculations(List<Sale>) via GetAllSalesByClientNetIdActor.cs:91: (a) both New gates (:556,:595) miss !IsPaymentBillDownloaded → New sale with downloaded bill shows dynamic totals on the client card vs frozen PricePerItem on the pivot; (b) USD non-New TotalAmount uses Product.CurrentPrice not PricePerItem (:577) so historical USD totals drift with today's price, and USD sales get no TotalAmountEurToUah; (c) no SaleInvoiceDocument shipping add-on (canonical :519-539) so sale totals exclude shipping the pivot includes. Console SalesPanel.tsx:418/:506/:573 displays TotalAmountLocal from this endpoint.
confirmed.expected: Match canonical plural helper (:444) including payment-bill gate, PricePerItem for non-New, and shipping add-on.
confirmed.explanation: Confirmed on sub-claim (a), which alone produces wrong data in a real, recurring scenario. Both New gates in CalculatePricingsForSalesWithDynamicPricesWithUsdCalculations (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:556 and :595) omit !sale.IsPaymentBillDownloaded, unlike every other copy of the math (canonical plural :454, single-sale :135, SQL CASE SaleRepository.cs:180/185, ReportService.cs:319). The state "New + IsPaymentBillDownloaded=1" is not transient: for VAT sales the bill-download flow (SalesActor.cs:2889-2953) freezes PricePerItem and sets the flag at :2946 while the lifecycle move to Packaging is commented out (:2897/:2909), so the sale stays New until a later operation. Live dev DB shows this window routinely lasts hours to days (gaps between BillDownloadDate and lifecycle update: 12-10,018 minutes on recent VAT sales; two flagged sales are in New right now). During that window /sales/all/client (GetAllSalesByClientNetIdActor.cs:91) recomputes totals from dynamic Product.CurrentPrice instead of the frozen PricePerItem, so the console client-card SalesPanel (TotalAmountLocal at SalesPanel.tsx:418/:506/:573) and legacy edit.client.sales.view.tsx:78 show totals that drift from the downloaded bill and contradict the pivot (/sales/all/filtered) and register (/sales/all/register, which uses the canonical helper at GetAllSalesByClientNetIdActor.cs:59) whenever the product price/shares/FX change after bill download. Sub-claims (b) and (c) are real textual divergences but latent on live data: the DB contains zero USD-agreement sales ever (35 USD agreements, 0 sales), so the CurrentPrice-based USD non-New branch (:576-578) and missing USD TotalAmountEurToUah never execute; and SaleInvoiceDocument has 0 rows (no Sale.SaleInvoiceDocumentID set), so the shipping add-on never fires even in the canonical helper — additionally this endpoint's repo query GetAllByClientNetIdFiltered (SaleRepository.cs:1568-1735) never joins or maps SaleInvoiceDocument, so shipping support would require a SQL change too. Missing EurToUah for USD agreements is also display-correct (2nd amount is EurToUah only for EUR-currency non-VAT agreements).
confirmed.fix: Server-side fix (benefits both console SalesPanel and legacy edit.client.sales.view): in /root/projects/gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Commercial.Actors/Actors/Sales/SaleActorsHelpers.cs, CalculatePricingsForSalesWithDynamicPricesWithUsdCalculations, add the payment-bill gate to both New checks — line 556: `if (sale.BaseLifeCycleStatus.SaleLifeCycleType.Equals(SaleLifeCycleType.New) && !sale.IsPaymentBillDownloaded)` and line 595 likewise, matching the canonical gate at :454. Optionally, for latent parity (zero current impact): change the USD non-New branch (:576-578) to use PricePerItem with the ==0 fallback to Product.CurrentPrice, mirroring canonical :477-495 (TotalAmountLocal via ExchangeRateAmount). Do NOT swap the actor call to the canonical helper wholesale: the USD special-casing (no EurToUah for USD agreements) matches display semantics, and the shipping block would be a no-op here since GetAllByClientNetIdFiltered does not load SaleInvoiceDocument (and the table is empty on live data).

confirmed.area: server SignalR sale push math copy
confirmed.claim: AddedSale/UpdatedSale push payload (copy at :247, sent :90/:101) computes non-New totals from Product.CurrentPrice/CurrentLocalPrice (:315-322) instead of frozen PricePerItem, and its New gate (:256) misses !IsPaymentBillDownloaded; legacy merges the pushed Sale straight into the active sale session (sale.products.view.tsx:1389,497-500) so totals silently diverge from /sales/get. Console is unaffected (uses push only as reload trigger).
confirmed.expected: Mirror the canonical single-sale helper math in the pushed payload.
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. HubsSenderActor.GetSaleStatisticByNetId (HubsSenderActor.cs:158-217) builds the AddedSale/UpdatedSale push from SaleRepository.GetByNetId (SaleRepository.cs:9490-9528), which hydrates Product.CurrentPrice/CurrentLocalPrice via dbo.GetCalculatedProductPriceWithSharesAndVat — confirmed in the live dev DB to return the CURRENT catalog price (ProductPricing + extra charge + group discount + one-time discount), never the frozen OrderItem.PricePerItem. The actor's private math copy (HubsSenderActor.cs:247-372) then computes the non-New branch totals from those dynamic prices (:315-325) instead of frozen PricePerItem/ExchangeRateAmount as the canonical helper does (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:157-187), and its New gate (:256) omits !sale.IsPaymentBillDownloaded (canonical :135). It also lacks the Packaged-status CurrentPrice=PricePerItem overwrite (canonical :169-172) and the final CurrentPrice=PricePerItem assignment (canonical :185). Pushes demonstrably fire for frozen sales: agreement switch on Bill sales (SalesActor.cs:2873), payment-invoice export — the flow that sets IsPaymentBillDownloaded (SalesActor.cs:2979), invoice export (:3302), item shift/merge (BaseSalesGetActor.cs:1031-1036). Legacy consumes the payload as data: sales.pivot.tsx:582-601 replaces the whole grid row with the pushed sale and :515-533 unshifts pushed new sales; sale.products.view.tsx:494-517 sets session.Sale = pushed sale in the active sale session. So after e.g. printing a payment bill, the legacy pivot row's TotalAmount/TotalAmountLocal/TotalVat silently flip from frozen-price values (what /sales/all/filtered and /sales/get show) to live-catalog values. Ground truth: dev DB has frozen sales diverging today (Sale 672156 Bill: frozen 57.40 vs live 51.66 EUR/unit; Sale 662133: 18.30 vs 23.79, +30%), and for UAH agreements pushed TotalAmountLocal uses today's EUR->UAH rate via CurrentLocalPrice instead of the frozen ExchangeRateAmount (51.5), diverging on every rate move; TotalVat (derived from TotalAmountLocal) is wrong too. Console is unaffected (RealtimeProvider.tsx:86-92 + SalesUkrainePage.tsx:483-493 use the push only as a reload trigger). Impact is display-only and transient (corrected on next full fetch; totals are EF-ignored and never persisted), hence medium.
confirmed.fix: Server-side fix in gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Communication.Actors/Actors/Communications/HubsSenderActor.cs, private CalculatePricingsForSaleWithDynamicPrices (:247-372), mirroring SaleActorsHelpers.CalculatePricingsForSaleWithDynamicPrices (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:125-224) while keeping the hub-specific Created/Updated timezone conversion: (1) line 256: change the gate to `sale.BaseLifeCycleStatus.SaleLifeCycleType.Equals(SaleLifeCycleType.New) && !sale.IsPaymentBillDownloaded`; (2) in the else branch replace lines 315-325 with the canonical frozen math: `orderItem.TotalAmount = decimal.Round(orderItem.PricePerItem * (decimal)orderItem.Qty, 14, AwayFromZero); orderItem.TotalAmountLocal = decimal.Round(decimal.Round(orderItem.PricePerItem * (decimal)orderItem.Qty, 14, AwayFromZero) * orderItem.ExchangeRateAmount, 14, AwayFromZero); if (sale.BaseLifeCycleStatus.SaleLifeCycleType.Equals(SaleLifeCycleType.Packaged)) { orderItem.Product.CurrentPrice = orderItem.PricePerItem; orderItem.Product.CurrentLocalPrice = orderItem.PricePerItem * orderItem.ExchangeRateAmount; } orderItem.Product.CurrentPriceEurToUah = orderItem.Product.CurrentPrice * currentExchangeRateEurToUah; orderItem.TotalAmountEurToUah = decimal.Round(orderItem.Product.CurrentPriceEurToUah * (decimal)orderItem.Qty, 14, AwayFromZero);` keeping the existing TotalVat block, then after it `orderItem.Product.CurrentPrice = decimal.Round(orderItem.PricePerItem, 14, AwayFromZero); orderItem.Product.CurrentLocalPrice = decimal.Round(orderItem.Product.CurrentLocalPrice, 14, AwayFromZero);` (replacing lines 334-335). The Communication.Actors project cannot call SaleActorsHelpers directly without adding a ProjectReference to Commercial.Actors (no cycle exists, but it drags a large dependency); the cleaner long-term option is hoisting the canonical helper into a shared project (e.g. Operations.Contracts EntityHelpers) and deleting both copies. No client-side change needed: the console already treats the push as a reload trigger, and the legacy client's merge behavior becomes correct once the pushed payload matches /sales/get math.

confirmed.area: server live endpoints on pre-dynamic-era pricing helpers
confirmed.claim: Three live endpoints still use ancient helpers with wrong TotalAmountLocal semantics: /sales/get/current/unmerged (BaseSalesGetActor.cs:1275) and /sales/get/shifted (:1166) use CalculatePricingsForSale (:53-123) — TotalAmountLocal = price×Qty×euro EUR→UAH rate for ALL agreements with no currency check (EUR agreements get UAH-scale numbers), TotalAmountEurToUah never set, no shipping; /sales/all/subclients/client (BaseSalesGetActor.cs:190) uses CalculatePricingsForSales (:226) whose USD branch writes the USD cross-rate amount into EUR-semantics TotalAmount (:376) and non-USD computes Local with the current-culture EUR→UAH rate for EUR agreements (:419). Console consumers currently read only booleans/EUR fields, so latent for console but live-wrong for legacy merge flow.
confirmed.expected: Route these endpoints through the fixed single-sale / canonical plural helpers so the two «current sale» endpoints agree and Local/EurToUah semantics hold.
confirmed.explanation: Core defect confirmed, but the finding's impact attribution is inverted. CalculatePricingsForSale (gba-server SaleActorsHelpers.cs:53-123) does compute TotalAmountLocal = price*Qty*EUR->UAH rate for ALL agreement currencies (SaleExchangeRate.Value = 51.5 verified in dev DB; fallback GetEuroExchangeRateByCurrentCulture also UAH 51.5), never sets TotalAmountEurToUah, and omits shipping; it serves live routes /sales/get/shifted (BaseSalesGetActor.cs:1166) and /sales/get/current/unmerged (:1275). However: (1) the "legacy merge flow" is NOT live-wrong — legacy merged.sales.tsx:97 and console MergedSalesDrawer.tsx:144-147 read the unmerged payload only as an existence boolean; (2) /sales/all/subclients/client (:190) is dead on both clients — legacy GetSubClientSalesAction is never dispatched and SUCCESS_GET_SUB_CLIENT_SALES has no reducer case; console never calls it; (3) the REAL live wrong-data path is the console, contrary to the finding's "latent for console": in the New Sale wizard, NewSaleClientStep.tsx:833-866 (openEditRow/openAudit) calls replaceRegistryRow() with the /sales/get/shifted statistic, overwriting a registry row whose amounts came from the canonical CalculatePricingsForSalesWithDynamicPrices (/sales/all/register, GetAllSalesByClientNetIdActor.cs:59-60); WizardClientRegistry.tsx:339 renders sale.TotalAmountLocal + currency code as the row amount (also sort accessor :156 and expanded items :735). For a EUR-agreement sale (129 exist on dev, e.g. Sale 672156 with SaleExchangeRate=51.5) the displayed amount jumps ~51.5x the moment the user opens the audit/edit act on that row, and shipping is dropped from the total for every swapped row. Legacy /sales/get/shifted consumers (edit.sale.view.tsx:313, shift.order.item.tsx:35) display only OrderItem.TotalAmount hard-labeled EUR, which the ancient helper computes correctly in EUR for non-New sales, so legacy is largely insulated.
confirmed.fix: Server-side (benefits both clients). In gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Commercial.Actors/Actors/Sales/SalesGetActors/BaseSalesGetActor.cs: (1) line 1166 (ProcessGetSaleByNetIdWithShiftedItemsMessage) replace SaleActorsHelpers.CalculatePricingsForSale(saleFromDb, connection, _exchangeRateRepositoriesFactory, _saleRepositoriesFactory) with SaleActorsHelpers.CalculatePricingsForSaleWithDynamicPrices(saleFromDb, _exchangeRateRepositoriesFactory.NewExchangeRateRepository(connection), _currencyRepositoriesFactory.NewCurrencyRepository(connection)) — GetByNetIdWithShiftedItems loads everything the canonical non-New branch needs (OrderItem.PricePerItem and persisted ExchangeRateAmount column verified in dev DB, Agreement.Organization joined at SaleRepository.cs:11276); (2) line 1275 (ProcessGetCurrentNotMergedSaleByClientAgreementMessage) make the same substitution, exactly mirroring the sibling /sales/get/current handler at lines 1236-1240 which already uses GetByNetId + the canonical helper, so the two "current sale" endpoints agree; (3) line 190 (/sales/all/subclients/client) is dead in both clients — either delete the endpoint or route it through CalculatePricingsForSalesWithDynamicPrices like line 209 for consistency. Caveat (no regression, note only): GetByNetIdWithShiftedItems does not project dynamic Product.CurrentPrice, so New-stage sales with unset PricePerItem still total 0 under either helper; full parity for that edge would require adding the dynamic-price projection used by GetByNetId.

confirmed.area: console editor header total recomputed client-side
confirmed.claim: For non-VAT EUR sales the header sums item.TotalAmountEurToUah over OrderItems (:215-217) instead of using sale.TotalAmountEurToUah (populated by /sales/get, SaleActorsHelpers.cs:223); before hydration completes or if /sales/get errors, OrderItems are absent and the header shows 0,00 UAH even though the row-level sale carries a correct total.
confirmed.expected: Use the sale-level TotalAmountEurToUah with the same fallback the non-EurToUah branch has (sale.TotalAmountLocal).
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. SaleEditorDrawer is opened from the online-shop grid (SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:729-731) with a row from /sales/all/filtered?includeDetails=false (salesOnlineShopApi.ts:11). On that server path SaleRepository.cs:2853-2854 loads no OrderItems but PopulateSalesListTotals sets sale-level TotalAmountEurToUah (SaleRepository.cs:251), and the list mapper attaches Agreement.WithVATAccounting + Currency.Code (SaleRepository.cs:2699-2727), so isNonVatEurSale(sale) is true immediately. SaleEditorDrawer.tsx:215-216 computes the header for non-VAT EUR sales by summing item.TotalAmountEurToUah over sale.Order.OrderItems, which is empty pre-hydration; the total renders unconditionally at :348 (loading only shows a spinner at :345, and on /sales/get failure the error alert at :314-318 renders alongside the header). Result: every editor open for a non-VAT EUR sale flashes "0,00 UAH" until getSaleById resolves, and shows it permanently if the fetch fails — while sale.TotalAmountEurToUah on the row payload already holds the correct value. The sibling branch (:217) already prefers sale-level fields, so the asymmetry is real. The fix is display-safe: SaleActorsHelpers.cs:192/:223 makes sale.TotalAmountEurToUah exactly equal the item sum after hydration (shipping is added only to TotalAmount/TotalAmountLocal at :214-217, never to EurToUah), and the sale-level field is already the established pattern in the grid (SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1869) and declared in types.ts:216. Severity low: transient/error-window wrong display only, no persisted data impact. Note: the similar item-sum in NewSaleReviewStep.tsx:321 is NOT affected — that sale is built client-side and has no sale-level total.
confirmed.fix: Console-side (server already supplies the value on both list and get paths). In /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/components/SaleEditorDrawer.tsx:215-217 make the EurToUah branch prefer the sale-level total, keeping the item-sum as fallback: `const headerTotal = useEurToUah ? getNumber(sale.TotalAmountEurToUah) ?? roundMoney(orderItems.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (getNumber(item.TotalAmountEurToUah) ?? 0), 0)) : getNumber(sale.TotalAmountLocal) ?? getNumber(sale.TotalAmount) ?? 0`. This mirrors the fallback style of the non-EurToUah branch and is numerically identical post-hydration.

confirmed.area: console discount modal sale-level comment prefill
confirmed.claim: Sale-level comment initial value falls back to orderItems[0]?.OneTimeDiscountComment when sale.OneTimeDiscountComment is empty (:53-54); legacy uses only Sale.OneTimeDiscountComment (sale.discount.modal.tsx:52) — an item-only comment can be silently promoted to the sale-level comment on save (persisted via UpdateDiscountComment, SalesActor.cs:1797-1799).
confirmed.expected: Prefill sale-level comment from Sale.OneTimeDiscountComment only.
confirmed.explanation: Console SaleDiscountModal.tsx:53-54 prefills the sale-level comment with orderItems[0]?.OneTimeDiscountComment when Sale.OneTimeDiscountComment is empty; legacy (sale.discount.modal.tsx:52) uses only Sale.OneTimeDiscountComment and, since the comment is required, forces the user to type one. On save the console's sale-level branch (lines 97-110) writes that prefilled comment to Sale.OneTimeDiscountComment and to EVERY order item, and the server persists it unconditionally (SalesActor.cs ProcessUpdateOneTimeDiscountsOnSaleMessage: sale.OneTimeDiscountComment = message.Sale.OneTimeDiscountComment; saleRepository.UpdateDiscountComment(sale); plus per-item UpdateOneTimeDiscount/UpdateOneTimeDiscountComment). The modal receives a fully hydrated sale via /sales/get (ensureSaleDetails, SalesUkrainePage.tsx:376-384), so OrderItems[].OneTimeDiscountComment is present and the fallback genuinely fires. Live dev DB confirms the triggering pre-state exists today: 3 sales (662087, 672156, 662154) have an empty sale-level comment while their FIRST order item carries one. Real failure: user sets a per-item comment, later opens the sale-level discount modal, enters a percent, saves — the item-only comment is silently promoted to the sale-level comment and overwrites all other items' comments; legacy could never do this without the user typing it. Wrong-source data defect, not cosmetic; low severity because the value is visible and editable in the textarea before save.
confirmed.fix: Console-side (server is legacy-faithful; both SalesUkrainePage and SalesOnlineShopPage share this modal). In /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/components/SaleDiscountModal.tsx lines 53-55, drop the item fallback: const [comment, setComment] = useState(() => orderItem ? orderItem.OneTimeDiscountComment || '' : sale.OneTimeDiscountComment || ''). The existing required-comment validation (line 66) then forces the user to type a sale-level comment, matching legacy.

confirmed.claim: Console-only summary card (:317-322, rendered :636-640): for non-VAT EUR sales it client-side sums item.TotalAmountEurToUah (excluding sale-level shipping) while for UAH/VAT sales it shows sale.TotalAmountLocal (including shipping) — two different bases for the same figure; and when splitItems exist it still shows the residual original invoice's count/total although submit creates the split накладна from splitItems.
confirmed.expected: Use sale.TotalAmountEurToUah for the EurToUah branch and show split totals in split mode.
confirmed.explanation: Split verdict on a bundled finding; the confirmable half stands. (A) "Mixed basis" REJECTED: the console's item-sum of TotalAmountEurToUah (NewSaleReviewStep.tsx:321) is arithmetically identical to the canonical sale.TotalAmountEurToUah — the server computes it as the same item sum (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:192,223) and never adds shipping to it (shipping block :198-218 touches only TotalAmount/TotalAmountLocal). Showing EurToUah for non-VAT EUR sales and TotalAmountLocal otherwise is exactly the legacy display rule. Moreover sale-level shipping comes only from SaleInvoiceDocument, which has 0 rows in the live dev DB and is populated only by the Poland invoice-builder flow (legacy sale.master.view.tsx:319-323, POLAND_LANGUAGE-gated, out of scope), so the two branches cannot numerically diverge for any real Ukraine sale. (B) Split-mode display CONFIRMED: split quantities are deducted from the original sale server-side during cart editing (NewSaleProductsStep.tsx:817-821 + onCartChanged), so at review time sale.Order.OrderItems is the residual invoice; the summary card (NewSaleReviewStep.tsx:317-322, rendered :636-640) shows the residual's line count and total, while submit with splitItems present (:526,:554) builds the payload from buildSplitSale(sale, splitItems) (:771-796) and createSale (:561) — creating a накладна with different items and a different total, and the carrier/recipient/COD picked on this same screen are applied to that split payload. The user confirming «Створити накладну» against the displayed figure sees the wrong document's count/total. Legacy's review step (sale.single.review.view.tsx) shows no totals summary at all, so this is not a legacy-faithful quirk but console-added UI populated from the wrong document in split mode. Display-only, rare flow, hence low severity.
confirmed.fix: Console owns the fix (pure display card, no server change). In /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/components/new-sale-wizard/NewSaleReviewStep.tsx, when splitItems.length > 0 derive the summary from splitItems instead of the residual sale: count = splitItems.length, and total = roundMoney(splitItems.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (useEurToUah ? item.TotalAmountEurToUah : item.TotalAmountLocal), 0)); keep the existing :320-322 logic for the non-split case. WizardSplitOrderItem already carries TotalAmountEurToUah and TotalAmountLocal (EditShoppingCartOverlay.tsx:11-19, computed in NewSaleProductsStep.tsx buildSplitItem/rebuildSplitItem :2444-2481), so no new data plumbing is needed. Do NOT change the EurToUah-vs-TotalAmountLocal branch selection itself — it matches server/legacy semantics; the "mixed basis"/shipping half of the finding needs no fix.

confirmed.area: console+legacy dead field OverLordQty vs server OverLoadQty
confirmed.claim: Console reads orderItem.OverLordQty (SaleExpandContent.tsx:114-117/194, sales-ukraine/types.ts:213, shopping-cart-reserve/types.ts:46 + utils.ts:101-105) but the server serializes OverLoadQty (OrderItem.cs:45 / OrderItemMap.cs:48) — the «Qty / OverQty» suffix, hasQtyOverflow and is-qty-warning highlight can never fire on sales-ukraine, sales-online-shop (shared expand component) or cart-reserve; legacy has the identical dead typo (entities/order.item.ts:23, sale.expand.content.item.tsx:109).
confirmed.expected: Read OverLoadQty in the console types/reads (reportable dead-field class: the behavior silently never works in either UI).
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. The server serializes the persisted property OverLoadQty (OrderItem.cs:44, OrderItemMap.cs:48, DB column added by migration 20251215160425_OverLoadQtyOrderItem) with PascalCase preserved on the wire (GbaJsonOptions.cs:15 PropertyNamingPolicy=null, no [JsonPropertyName] rename), and the Dapper hydration SQL selects [OrderItem].* (SaleRepository.cs:497 and in GetByNetId at :9490+), so the payload field is exactly "OverLoadQty". The console reads a nonexistent field: sales-ukraine/types.ts:213 and shopping-cart-reserve/types.ts:46 type OverLordQty; SaleExpandContent.tsx:114 does getNumber(orderItem.OverLordQty) which returns null for undefined (helper at :292), so the "Qty / OverQty" suffix (:116), hasQtyOverflow (:117), the is-qty-warning row class (:122) and the warning ValueBlock (:194) can never fire on sales-ukraine or sales-online-shop (shared component); same for shopping-cart-reserve/utils.ts:104 formatQty. Legacy is identically dead: entities/order.item.ts:23 is a bare typed class with no JSON-key remapping, so sale.expand.row.tsx:15 and sale.expand.content.item.tsx:109 read undefined. This is precisely the reportable dead-field class (behavior silently never works in either UI). Caveat that keeps severity low: the write side is equally dead — neither client ever assigns/sends the field, so ClientShoppingCartService.cs:319/447 UpdateOverLoadQty always persists 0; live dev DB confirms 0 of 1,885,368 OrderItem rows have OverLoadQty<>0 (and OrderService.cs:212-215 OverLordTotalAmount is consequently always 0). Renaming the client reads makes the display correct the moment any writer populates OverLoadQty, but today no data would trigger it.
confirmed.fix: Fix on the client side (do NOT rename the server property — OverLoadQty is the persisted DB column wired through Dapper INSERT/UPDATE strings in OrderItemRepository.cs:37/40/128, and aliasing the wire name to the typo would entrench it). Console: rename OverLordQty -> OverLoadQty in /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/types.ts:213, /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/components/SaleExpandContent.tsx:114, /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/shopping-cart-reserve/types.ts:46 and utils.ts:104. Legacy (if still maintained): same rename in /root/projects/gba_client/src/entities/order.item.ts:23, sale.expand.row.tsx:15, sale.expand.content.item.tsx:109. Companion gap worth a follow-up ticket: nothing ever populates OverLoadQty (all rows 0) because neither client sends it on cart add/update — decide whether the server should compute it (e.g. requested-vs-available capping in ClientShoppingCartService) or the clients should submit it; until then the warning UI will correctly compile but still never display.

confirmed.claim: code = client?.RegionCode?.Value always uses the sale client's own code; legacy shows the RootClient's RegionCode when RootClient exists (sale.item.client.name.tsx:49-51) and the list payload provides it (SalesController.cs:871-887).
confirmed.expected: Use client.RootClient?.RegionCode?.Value ?? client.RegionCode?.Value.
confirmed.explanation: Console SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1130 always renders the sale client's own RegionCode (client?.RegionCode?.Value), while legacy (sale.item.client.name.tsx:49-51) renders RootClient.RegionCode whenever RootClient exists. The /sales/all/filtered payload the console consumes does carry ClientAgreement.Client.RootClient.RegionCode.Value (SalesController.cs GET_ALL_FILTERED projection ~871-887, hydrated via SaleRepository.cs:2784-2832 RootClient/RootRegionCode LEFT JOIN), and the console's own SalesUkraineClient type (types.ts:107-112) already declares the field — it is simply unused. Live dev DB confirms real impact: 255 non-deleted sales have a sub-client code differing from the root's (e.g., Sale 662131 shows BCE00605 in console vs CE00600 in legacy). The same page already applies the root-client rule for the display name (getSaleClientDisplayName, lines 1738-1748), making the code column internally inconsistent too. Display-only list column, hence low severity.
confirmed.fix: Console-side fix (server already supplies both codes; legacy performs this selection client-side as well). In /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/pages/SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1130 replace `const code = client?.RegionCode?.Value?.trim()` with the legacy-faithful branch on RootClient presence: `const code = (client?.RootClient ? client.RootClient.RegionCode?.Value : client?.RegionCode?.Value)?.trim()`. Note: do NOT use `RootClient?.RegionCode?.Value ?? client.RegionCode?.Value` — legacy shows nothing (not the sub-client's code) when a RootClient exists without a RegionCode.

confirmed.explanation: CONFIRMED end-to-end. The /sales/all/filtered includeDetails=false payload consumed by SalesUkrainePage carries RetailClient: SaleRepository.GetAllRangedByLifeCycleType LEFT JOINs [RetailClient] and assigns sale.RetailClient (gba-server SaleRepository.cs:2628-2629, 2742), and ToSaleListItem serializes {Name, PhoneNumber, FullName} (SalesController.cs:969-977). Yet the row client block (SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1242-1262, fed by getSaleClientName/getSaleClientDisplayName at 1727-1749) and the detail-sheet «Клієнт і договір» section (1495-1503) render only ClientAgreement.Client/RootClient — the retail buyer is never shown. confirmed.fix: Console-side only (server already sends the data). In /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/pages/SalesUkrainePage.tsx: (1) add a getRetailClientLine(sale) helper mirroring SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:1405-1413 (return [sale.RetailClient?.PhoneNumber, sale.RetailClient?.Name || sale.RetailClient?.FullName].filter(Boolean).join(' - '), empty string when no RetailClient); (2) in the row client block (~line 1250, inside .sg-client-name after the client name span) render the retail line when non-empty, e.g. a muted span `{retailLine}` matching legacy's appended `PhoneNumber (Name)`; (3) in the SaleDetail «Клієнт і договір» section (~line 1499) add a row [t('Покупець'), getRetailClientLine(sale)] (SaleDetailSection already skips empty values, or add conditionally). Optionally extract the helper into a shared module used by both sales-online-shop and sales-ukraine to avoid the duplicate.

confirmed.area: console phone tooltip loses list-projection alias after hydration
confirmed.claim: Reads Client?.MobileNumber || Client?.PhoneNumber (also SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:1087); PhoneNumber is a list-projection alias for Client.ClientNumber (SalesController.cs:860) that does not exist on the entity serialized by /sales/get (Client.cs:118 has ClientNumber only) — the value disappears when the row is replaced post-hydration.
confirmed.expected: Read ClientNumber (or keep the alias merged) so the phone survives hydration.
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. The console detail sheet always renders the /sales/get-hydrated sale: list rows carry HasDetails=false (SalesController.cs:778), so needsSaleDetails (SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1884) forces getSaleById before the sheet opens (SalesUkrainePage.tsx:336-374), and replaceSaleInList (1888) wholesale-replaces the row. /sales/get returns the raw Sale entity graph (GetSaleByNetIdActor.cs:38-55, SaleRepository.cs:9490/9542 selects [Client].*) serialized PascalCase with no naming policy (GbaJsonOptions.cs:15); the Client entity has MobileNumber (Client.cs:116) and ClientNumber (Client.cs:118) but no PhoneNumber property at all — PhoneNumber exists only as the list-projection alias PhoneNumber = ClientNumber (SalesController.cs:860), which the sheet never sees. Therefore Client?.PhoneNumber at SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1502 and SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:1087 is always undefined (dead fallback, same class as OverLordQty). Live dev DB impact: 450 of 531 distinct clients in 2026 sales (85%) have empty MobileNumber but populated ClientNumber, so the «Телефон» row renders blank for most real sales despite the phone being present in the payload as ClientNumber. Minor wording correction: the value never renders pre-hydration and then vanishes — openSaleSheet awaits hydration first, so the fallback simply never fires. Ownership is console-side: legacy never reads Client.PhoneNumber (its uses are DeliveryRecipient.MobilePhone and RetailClient.PhoneNumber, a real FastClient column), so a server entity alias would only serve the console.
confirmed.fix: Console-side (both pages): append ClientNumber as the final fallback. In /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/pages/SalesUkrainePage.tsx:1502 and /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-online-shop/pages/SalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:1087 change the row value to sale.ClientAgreement?.Client?.MobileNumber || sale.ClientAgreement?.Client?.PhoneNumber || sale.ClientAgreement?.Client?.ClientNumber (keeping PhoneNumber preserves the list-projection alias for the rare unhydrated edge case where getSaleById returns empty and the list row is used). Add ClientNumber?: string to SalesUkraineClient in /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-ukraine/types.ts (type at lines 95-113) and to the corresponding client type in /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/sales-online-shop/types.ts. No server change: legacy does not consume Client.PhoneNumber, so adding an alias property to the Client entity would broaden the payload of every Client-serializing endpoint for a console-only display concern.

confirmed.claim: Renders item.TotalWeight (mapped IncompleteSalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:811), but OrderItem.TotalWeight is EF-ignored (OrderItemMap.cs:66) and MisplacedSaleRepository.GetAllFiltered (:325-448) never computes it — always «0,000 Вага»; legacy equally dead (order.item.row.tsx:52).
confirmed.expected: Drop the weight line for incomplete sales or compute it server-side.
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. Console /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/clients/components/IncompleteSaleItemsList.tsx:92 renders formatWeight(item.TotalWeight) («Вага»), fed from the raw payload via toSaleOrderItem at /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/incomplete-sales-online-shop/pages/IncompleteSalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:811. The payload comes from /sales/misplaced/get/all → MisplacedSaleActor.cs:36 → MisplacedSaleRepository.GetAllFiltered (/root/projects/gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Operations.Infrastructure.Persistence/Sales/Repositories/MisplacedSaleRepository.cs:325-448), whose mapper sets orderItem.TotalAmount/TotalAmountLocal (lines 359-360, 386-387) but never TotalWeight; GetBySaleNetId (:42-136, backs /sales/misplaced/get) and GetAll (:227-323) are identical. OrderItem.TotalWeight (OrderItem.cs:59) is EF-ignored (OrderItemMap.cs:66) and the live OrderItem table has no weight column (verified via sys.columns on dev ConcordDb_V5), so Dapper's [OrderItem].* can never hydrate it — it always serializes as 0. Repo-wide grep confirms no server code path ever assigns OrderItem.TotalWeight a computed value. Real-data proof: a dev misplaced-sale item has Product.Weight=1.85, Qty=1 (real weight 1.85 kg) yet would display «0,000 Вага»; 73,065 non-deleted products have Weight>0. Legacy is equally dead (incomplete.sale.item.tsx renders order.item.row.tsx:52 FormatNumber.Weight(OrderItem.TotalWeight) from the same endpoint), which places this squarely in the OverLordQty-class exception: a rendered metric that silently never shows real data in either client.
confirmed.fix: Fix on the server so both clients benefit: in /root/projects/gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Operations.Infrastructure.Persistence/Sales/Repositories/MisplacedSaleRepository.cs add orderItem.TotalWeight = product.Weight * orderItem.Qty; next to the existing TotalAmount/TotalAmountLocal assignments in both mapper branches of GetAllFiltered (after lines 360 and 387), GetBySaleNetId (after lines 74 and 94), and GetAll (after lines 261 and 283) — matching the canonical sale-level semantics (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:514: Product.Weight * Qty). No console change needed; alternatively, if product weight is deemed meaningless for incomplete online-shop carts, drop the weight line at IncompleteSaleItemsList.tsx:88-93 instead — but the server computation is the preferred minimal fix since legacy order.item.row.tsx:52 then starts working too.

confirmed.expected: Remove the author line or have the server populate OrderItem.User.
confirmed.explanation: Dead-field case verified end-to-end. Console IncompleteSaleItemsList.tsx:74-78 renders item.User?.LastName, fed by IncompleteSalesOnlineShopPage.tsx:793/812 (route /sales/misplaced/get/all) and RetailIncompleteSalePage.tsx:71-74 (route /sales/misplaced/get). Both routes resolve through MisplacedSaleActor.cs:34-50 to MisplacedSaleRepository.cs, where every multi-map mapper (GetBySaleNetId :56-99, GetAll :243-290, GetAllFiltered :341-393) assigns the joined [User] (joined on MisplacedSale.UserID, :420-421) only to misplacedSale.User (:80-82, :267-269, :368-370) and never to orderItem.User, so the serialized OrderItem.User is always null and the author line never renders. This matches the explicitly in-scope OverLordQty class: UI behavior that silently never works.
confirmed.fix: Console-side removal (server cannot own this fix: OrderItem.UserID is NULL for all misplaced-sale items, so there is nothing meaningful to populate, and the misplaced-sale-level responsible user is already shown as the Відповідальний field in the page header). In /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/clients/components/IncompleteSaleItemsList.tsx delete the dead block at lines 74-78 ({item.User?.LastName && (<Text ...>{item.User.LastName}</Text>)}); in /root/projects/gba_console/src/features/incomplete-sales-online-shop/pages/IncompleteSalesOnlineShopPage.tsx drop the dead mapping at line 793 (const user = item.User ...) and line 812 (User: user) from toSaleOrderItem. Leave the SaleOrderItem type's User field intact if referenced by other features.

confirmed.claim: ProcessUpdateMergedSaleToBillMessage Forwards GetSaleByNetIdMessage (reply to HTTP caller) BEFORE SaveExchangeRates (:1742) and SavePricesPerItem (:1744) run; for the just-converted Bill (non-New) the fixed helper multiplies PricePerItem which may still be 0 → intermittent zero totals/ExchangeRateAmount in the response (console ignores the response, salesUkraineApi.ts:216-221; legacy renders it).
confirmed.expected: Forward the reply only after prices/rates are persisted.
confirmed.explanation: The race is real and worse than claimed, but its blast radius is smaller than claimed. ProcessUpdateMergedSaleToBillMessage (SalesActor.cs:1578) uses a plain autocommit connection (no transaction), and the Forward of GetSaleByNetIdMessage at SalesActor.cs:1727 dispatches to GetSaleByNetIdActor — a RoundRobinPool(10) with its own connection (SalesManagementActor.cs:58, GetSaleByNetIdActor.cs:35) — BEFORE SetDebtToClient (:1731), SaveExchangeRates (:1742) and SavePricesPerItem (:1744). The new bill's OrderItems are inserted with PricePerItem=0/ExchangeRateAmount=0 (built at :1651-1658 with only Qty/ProductId/Vat, persisted via CreateNewBill :3975 through OrderItemRepository INSERT which writes PricePerItem), and the lifecycle status is flipped to Packaging before the Forward (:1683-1686), so the reader's CalculatePricingsForSaleWithDynamicPrices takes the non-New branch (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:157-188) and multiplies the persisted zeros: the SaleStatistic returned as the HTTP 200 body (SalesController.cs:663-679) carries TotalAmount/TotalAmountLocal=0. Real prices are persisted only at SavePricesPerItem's OrderItemRepository.Update (:3539), after SetDebtToClient; since the reader's work is a strict subset of the writer's remaining work, the response is wrong nearly deterministically, not just intermittently. However, the candidate's impact statement is half wrong: the console ignores the body (gba_console salesUkraineApi.ts:216-221), and legacy does NOT render it either — SUCCESS_UPDATE_MERGED_SALES_API is dispatched with the payload (gba_client/src/web.api/sale.api.ts:486) but no reducer in gba_client/src handles that action type, so the payload is dropped. Residual real harms that justify CONFIRMED at low severity: (a) premature completion signal — console callers reload immediately after the await (MergedSalesDrawer.tsx:247-251, NewSaleReviewStep.tsx:539-549) and the re-fetch races the still-unsaved prices, so refreshed list totals (SQL PopulateSalesListTotals reads persisted PricePerItem) can transiently show zeros; (b) error masking — an exception in SetDebtToClient/SaveExchangeRates/SavePricesPerItem after the Forward reaches catch Sender.Tell(exc) (:1779) only after the Ask already completed with the success reply, so the client gets 200 OK for a half-failed conversion; (c) any direct API consumer of /sales/update/merged receives zero totals. Note the identical Forward-before-save ordering exists in the non-merged update-to-bill path (SalesActor.cs:1511 before :1524/:1526) — a separate follow-up candidate, not ruled here.
confirmed.fix: Server-side, in /root/projects/gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Commercial.Actors/Actors/Sales/SalesActor.cs ProcessUpdateMergedSaleToBillMessage: move the line `ActorReferenceManager.Instance.Get(SalesActorNames.GET_SALE_BY_NET_ID_ACTOR).Forward(new GetSaleByNetIdMessage(newSale.NetUid));` from :1727 to after SavePricesPerItem completes and the client-registration/temporary-client updates run — i.e., after :1754, immediately before `if (!saleFromDb.OutputSaleMerges.Any()) return;` (:1756). The existing re-read `newSale = saleRepository.GetByNetId(newSale.NetUid);` at :1729 stays where it is (it feeds SetDebtToClient/SavePricesPerItem), so the move is a pure reorder with no other changes. This makes the forwarded reader see persisted PricePerItem/ExchangeRateAmount (correct totals in the response), makes the HTTP call complete only after the bill's financial state is durable (fixing the console's transient zero-totals reload race), and lets the catch's Sender.Tell(exc) actually reach the caller when debt/price persistence fails. The trailing output-sale item adjustments (:1758-1777) only touch the OUTPUT merged sale, not the returned bill, so replying before them is acceptable for a minimal fix. Consider the same reorder for the non-merged path (:1511 Forward vs :1524/:1526 saves) as a follow-up.

confirmed.claim: GetOrderItemAndSaleStatistics fetches the item from saleTo (:1040) but computes CurrentLocalPrice/TotalAmountLocal with GetEuroSaleExchangeRateBySaleNetId(saleFrom.NetUid) (:1042-1052) — the SOURCE sale's frozen euro rate with no agreement-currency check (EUR agreement → UAH-scale Local) — and returns no EurToUah; console ignores the response (newSaleWizardApi.ts:229) so impact is latent.
confirmed.expected: Use the destination item's own ExchangeRateAmount / the fixed single-sale helper.
confirmed.explanation: CONFIRMED, with one impact correction: the response is NOT fully latent — the legacy client renders it. Verified: BaseSalesGetActor.cs ProcessGetOrderItemAndSaleStatisticsMessage loads the DESTINATION item via GetBySaleNetIdAndOrderItemId(message.SaleTo, ...) (:1039-1040), which populates only EUR CurrentPrice/TotalAmount (OrderItemRepository.cs:1800-1948), then multiplies by GetEuroSaleExchangeRateBySaleNetId(saleFrom.NetUid) (:1042) — the SOURCE sale's frozen EUR→UAH rate — unconditionally (:1044-1052; fallback is the current-culture EUR→UAH rate, same scale), and never sets TotalAmountEurToUah/CurrentPriceEurToUah. Live dev DB confirms SaleExchangeRate(Code='EUR').Value=51.5 for BOTH EUR- and UAH-agreement sales while OrderItem.ExchangeRateAmount=1.0 for EUR agreements, so a EUR-agreement destination gets TotalAmountLocal ≈51.5x too large. The canonical sibling handler in the same file (:977-991) uses orderItem.ExchangeRateAmount and sets EurToUah — this shift path is a divergent copy of the math. Consumption: console discards the body (gba_console newSaleWizardApi.ts:224-234, Promise<void>) so it is latent there, but legacy dispatches the body as SUCCESS_NEW_ORDER_ITEM (gba_client order.api.ts:247-252) → product.reducer.ts:221-222 NewOrderItem → sale.products.shopping.card.base.view.tsx:246-260 inserts/replaces the row in the open destination-sale cart → ShoppingCartGrid.tsx:117/121 renders exactly these fields. Real manifestations until the sale view is reloaded: EUR agreement with VAT accounting shows UAH-scale (~51.5x) numbers in the local/EUR columns; EUR agreement without VAT (withUah, sale.products.view.tsx:231) renders the never-set *EurToUah fields as 0.00; UAH agreement uses the source sale's frozen rate instead of the destination's (wrong when the two sales froze different rates). Transient (recomputed on next fetch, never persisted), so severity low.
confirmed.fix: Server-owned fix in gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Commercial.Actors/Actors/Sales/SalesGetActors/BaseSalesGetActor.cs:1042-1052 (ProcessGetOrderItemAndSaleStatisticsMessage), mirroring the canonical math at :977-991: (1) derive the local rate from the DESTINATION sale/agreement — use orderItem.ExchangeRateAmount when non-zero; otherwise 1.0 when saleTo.ClientAgreement.Agreement.Currency.Code == "EUR", else GetEuroSaleExchangeRateBySaleNetId(saleTo.NetUid) (fallback GetEuroExchangeRateByCurrentCulture) — and compute CurrentLocalPrice/TotalAmountLocal with that rate; (2) additionally set orderItem.Product.CurrentPriceEurToUah = CurrentPrice * current EUR→UAH rate and orderItem.TotalAmountEurToUah = TotalAmount * same rate so the legacy withUah cart columns are not 0. No client change needed: legacy already renders whatever the server returns, and the console ignores the body.

confirmed.claim: Private copy gates on New only (missing !IsPaymentBillDownloaded, canonical :454) and rounds Order.TotalAmountLocal to 2dp (:238) where item sums are 14dp elsewhere; consumers: retail-client sales and payment-images lists (:68,:143).
confirmed.expected: Match the canonical gate and precision.
confirmed.explanation: The private copy is a stale fork of the canonical helper and its gate divergence is real and reachable. BaseRetailClientGetActor.cs:181 branches to dynamic pricing on `SaleLifeCycleType.New` alone, while every other copy of this math gates on `New && !IsPaymentBillDownloaded` (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:454 plural, :135 single, :704 even has a dedicated `New && IsPaymentBillDownloaded` branch; ReportService.cs:319; and the SQL totals in SaleRepository.cs:180 use `SaleLifeCycleType = 0 AND IsPaymentBillDownloaded = 0`). The flag matters because bill download freezes prices: SalesActor.cs ProcessExportInvoiceForPaymentForSaleByNetIdForPrintingMessage persists PricePerItem/ExchangeRateAmount via SavePricesPerItem (:2917) then sets IsPaymentBillDownloaded=true (:2946) while the lifecycle transition to Packaging is commented out (:2897/:2909) — so New+flag=1 is a real state (dev DB has 2 flagged sales; the :704 dedicated branch proves the combination is expected). That path requires IsVatSale, and retail sales qualify: in dev, all 17 sales with RetailClientId set have IsVatSale=1. Consumers of the private copy are RetailClientController.cs:66 (retail-client card sales list) and SalesController.cs:1732 GetAllPaymentImagesFilteredMessage (payment-images screen). For a New sale with a downloaded bill, these two endpoints recompute totals from live Product.CurrentPrice (current price list/discounts/FX) instead of the frozen billed PricePerItem, so after any price/rate drift they show amounts that disagree with the sales pivot, sale details, and the printed bill. Secondary confirmed divergence: :238 rounds Order.TotalAmountLocal to 2dp (canonical :511 uses 14dp) and :243 derives TotalVat from that pre-rounded value — sub-cent only, but eliminated by the same fix. Severity low: narrow surface (two retail screens), fires only in the New+bill-downloaded window with subsequent price drift, and current dev data has no live retail sales (all 17 are Deleted=1). Caveat: no live wrong row exists in dev today; the defect is deterministic once the modeled state recurs.
confirmed.fix: Server-side (both retail endpoints benefit; no client change needed). In /root/projects/gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Commercial.Actors/Actors/Clients/RetailClientGetActors/BaseRetailClientGetActor.cs: delete the private CalculatePricingsForSalesWithDynamicPrices (lines 169-272) and have the two call sites (:68 and :143) call the canonical SaleActorsHelpers.CalculatePricingsForSalesWithDynamicPrices (public static, same assembly; add `using Global.Business.Assistant.Actors.Sales;`). Behavioral deltas are exactly the desired ones: correct `New && !IsPaymentBillDownloaded` gate, 14dp Order.TotalAmountLocal, plus harmless TotalWeight population. Minimal alternative if the fork must stay: append `&& !sale.IsPaymentBillDownloaded` to the condition at line 181 and change the rounding at line 238 from 2 to 14 decimal places.

confirmed.area: server analytics/history snapshots use dynamic price for invoiced events
confirmed.claim: The live OrderItemHistory writers in ReportService (Reserve/SetLastStep/ShiftCurrent blocks; the copy called at :308 itself is dead — only caller commented out at :147) snapshot TotalAmount/PricePerItem/CurrentPriceEurTo from Product.CurrentPrice, i.e. TODAY's dynamic price, even for Bill/Invoice-stage events (ActEditTheInvoice), not the sale's actual frozen PricePerItem — analytics rows misstate historical amounts when prices changed.
confirmed.expected: Snapshot frozen PricePerItem-based values for non-New/billed-stage history events.
confirmed.explanation: Verified end-to-end. ReportService.SetLastStep (ReportService.cs:204-218) and ShiftCurrent (:247-264) write OrderHistory.TotalAmount(Local) and OrderItemHistory.{TotalAmount, TotalAmountLocal, PricePerItem, CurrentPriceEurTo} from Product.CurrentPrice/CurrentLocalPrice. That CurrentPrice is populated by SaleRepository.GetByNetId (SaleRepository.cs:9519-9528) via dbo.GetCalculatedProductPriceWithSharesAndVat, whose full body (read from dev DB) computes from CURRENT ProductPricing.Price + current extra-charge/discounts — it never returns the frozen OrderItem.PricePerItem. These two writers fire for post-freeze stages (InvoiceType.TransferToConsignment / ActEditTheInvoice = Акт редагування), where every canonical copy of the math uses frozen PricePerItem: ReportService's own else-branch (:342-360), SaleActorsHelpers, and the rebuilt V5 report projection (SalesReportProjectionRepository.cs:299 SUM(oi.PricePerItem*Qty) AS Agg_ValueWithVat). The retired pivot that consumed these rows summed OrderItemHistory.TotalAmount AS SalesValueWithVAT (ReportSaleRepository.cs:759), i.e. the rows represent sales value — so any ProductPricing/discount change between billing and the event permanently persists misstated amounts. The path is live: HistoryOrderItemController.cs:71/:89/:330 → legacy gba_client history.api.ts:36/:48/:72/:84; ConcordDb_Data holds real rows (SetLastStep x19, ActEditTheInvoice x3, latest 2026-06-29). Claim details also check out: the static at ReportService.cs:308 is dead (sole caller commented at :147-148), and SetReserve at New stage legitimately matches the New-branch dynamic-price semantics (:319-339). Severity stays low: after the analytics rebuild the tables are currently write-only (ReportSaleRepository.GetById/GetInfo have no live callers), and dev samples show only decimal(18,2) storage rounding because no price drift occurred between freeze and event in that data — the misstatement materializes only when prices change in the gap, which is routine in prod (daily 1C price sync).
confirmed.fix: Server-side only, in /root/projects/gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Application.Services/Services/Reports/ReportService.cs, SetLastStep (:204-218) and ShiftCurrent (:247-264) blocks: mirror the billed-stage canonical math (:342-360) — use decimal price = orderItemDb.PricePerItem != 0 ? orderItemDb.PricePerItem : orderItemDb.Product.CurrentPrice; set OrderItemHistory.PricePerItem = round(price), TotalAmount = round(price*Qty), TotalAmountLocal = round(round(price*Qty)*orderItemDb.ExchangeRateAmount) (agreement currency), and compute OrderHistory.TotalAmount/TotalAmountLocal as the sums of those item values. Leave CurrentPriceEurTo as Product.CurrentPrice * currentExchangeRateEurToUah (EurToUah display intentionally uses dynamic price per today's canonical fix). SetReserve (:128-142) needs no change (New-stage dynamic price is canonical). No client change needed. Alternative worth flagging to the user: since the OrderItemHistory pivot readers are dead after the analytics rebuild, these three ReportService writer calls could instead be retired entirely — but that is a product decision, not the minimal fix.

rejected.claim: Non-USD branch of CalculatePricingsForSaleWithDynamicPricesWithUsdCalculations(Sale,...) gates dynamic pricing on `New && sale.IsPaymentBillDownloaded` — the exact inverse of canonical `New && !IsPaymentBillDownloaded` (:135,:454). Callers SalesActor.cs:3231/:3267 (bill-download → invoice XLSX): any RE-download after price/discount changes regenerates the invoice at today's dynamic CurrentPrice instead of frozen PricePerItem; a Paid New sale never billed falls into the PricePerItem branch and prints 0.

rejected.explanation: The gate at SaleActorsHelpers.cs:704 is indeed inverted relative to the canonical helpers (:135, :454 use New && !IsPaymentBillDownloaded), but end-to-end tracing of all 9 call sites shows it is behavior-neutral in every reachable scenario. (1) All seven BaseSalesGetActor call sites (:246, :336, :447, :571, :648, :738, :898) reject New sales upfront with "Sale should be in invoice stage." (:243, :444, :645, :895), so the New check short-circuits to false and both gate directions take the identical frozen-PricePerItem branch. (2) The two cited callers SalesActor.cs:3231/:3267 run after the FromLastStep handler forcibly transitions every New VAT sale to Packaging and persists+refetches it: unbilled New sales via :3077-3143 (which also freezes PricePerItem via SavePricesPerItem at :3115) and billed New sales via :3146-3189; saleForInvoice fetched at :3227 is therefore never New for VAT sales. (3) Non-VAT sales can reach the helper as New and the gate direction then does alter the computed prices, but xlsxDocument is only assigned inside the IsVatSale block (:3216), so SalesController.cs:1458-1471 returns all-empty URLs and discards the generated invoice — no user ever receives the wrongly priced document, and the persisted side effects (SaleInvoiceNumber, IsPrintedPaymentInvoice) are gate-independent. (4) Both claimed failure scenarios are contradicted: a re-download reaches the helper with the sale in Packaging (frozen prices either way), and a Paid New never-billed VAT sale gets its prices frozen at :3115 and transitioned to Packaging before the helper, so it prints frozen prices, not 0. (5) Live dev DB confirms zero sales in state New + IsPaymentBillDownloaded=1. This is a latent code-hygiene landmine, not an active data defect.
rejected.fix: Optional hardening (behavior-neutral today): in gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Commercial.Actors/Actors/Sales/SaleActorsHelpers.cs:704, change the condition to sale.BaseLifeCycleStatus.SaleLifeCycleType.Equals(SaleLifeCycleType.New) && !sale.IsPaymentBillDownloaded to match the canonical gates at :135/:454, so any future caller that passes a New sale gets correct frozen-vs-dynamic selection. rejected.area: console retail payment guard before «Зробити рахунок»
rejected.claim: Guard pushes a blocking issue when /retail/clients/paid/amount returns a record with Id==0 or the fetch fails (used SaleEditorDrawer.tsx:219/241/541), permanently disabling conversion; legacy blocks only when RetailPaymentStatus.Id != 0 && Amount <= 0 (sale.single.review.view.tsx:439-443) — a missing payment record was allowed.

rejected.explanation: The divergence exists in code but never produces wrong behavior for a real scenario. (1) The finding's premise is impossible: /retail/clients/paid/amount can never return a record with Id==0 — RetailPaymentStatusRepository.GetBySaleId (RetailPaymentStatusRepository.cs:56-67) returns null when no active RetailClientPaymentImage exists, and RetailClientActor.cs:49 then does Sender.Tell(null), which throws InvalidMessageException in Akka.NET so the Ask never completes and the HTTP call errors/times out; the only reachable divergent branch in the console guard is 'fetch failed'. (2) In every data state that actually occurs, console == legacy: retail sales are always created together with a payment image + status (OrderService.cs:858-868, ~1123); live dev DB confirms 17/17 retail sales have exactly one image whose Deleted flag mirrors the sale's, zero live retail sales lack an active record, and RetailClientPaymentImageRepository.Remove has no callers, so no flow can orphan a live sale's payment record. With a record present, both clients block iff Amount<=0 (identical predicates). (3) On the error-only divergent path, the console displays the fetch error (SaleEditorDrawer.tsx:514-517), keeps the state retryable (retailPaymentKey includes reloadKey; reopening the drawer refetches), and fails safe on a payment gate — whereas legacy (sale.single.review.view.tsx:438-443 with default Id=0 state and a catch-less epic at sale.api.ts:965-983) silently allows converting a possibly-unpaid retail sale. This is deliberate, visible, recoverable hardening, not wrong gating, and per the brief such intentional console differences are rejected. The genuinely questionable code is server-side and pre-existing for both clients: the Tell(null) path means the endpoint cannot cleanly signal 'no record'.
rejected.fix: No console change needed. If the team ever wants a clean 'no record' signal instead of an Ask timeout, fix the server: in RetailClientActor.ProcessGetRetailClientPayedAmountBySaleIdMessage (RetailClientActor.cs:47-50), replace Sender.Tell(repo.GetBySaleId(id)) with a null-coalesced reply, e.g. Sender.Tell((object)(repo.GetBySaleId(message.SaleId) ?? new RetailPaymentStatus())); only then, to restore legacy fail-open, the console guard (saleReviewGuards.ts:52) would treat Id==0 as pass. Both changes are optional policy alignment, not defect fixes.

rejected.fix: No fix required for correctness today. rejected.area: console wizard carousel reservation price chain (dead fields)
rejected.claim: getReservationPrice(r) = r.Price ?? r.PricePerItem and the snapshot match r.ProductNetUid === productNetUid (:2505-2507, also :455,:511-512; types newSaleWizardApi.ts:33-45), but /products/reservations/current/carousel/agreement returns server ProductReservation which has NO Price, NO PricePerItem, NO ProductNetUid (price lives at OrderItem.PricePerItem) — the reservation step of the price chain can never fire and the find() never matches (masked by pricings/current and CurrentPrice fallbacks).

rejected.explanation: Factually accurate but not a data defect. Verified end-to-end: the endpoint (ProductReservationsController.cs:53 -> GetCurrentReservationsByProductAndClientAgreementNetIdsActor -> ProductReservationRepository.GetAllCurrentReservationsByProductNetId, ProductReservationRepository.cs:312-427) serializes raw ProductReservation entities (gba-server/.../Entities/Products/ProductReservation.cs:7-31) whose only fields are Qty, OrderItemId, ProductAvailabilityId, ConsignmentItemId, IsReSaleReservation, RegionCode, OrderItem (+EntityBase). Top-level Price/PricePerItem/ProductNetUid/AvailableQty can never appear in the payload, so the find() at NewSaleProductsStep.tsx:455 never matches and getReservationPrice (:2505-2507) always returns undefined — the candidate's mechanics are all true. However: (1) the dead terms sit inside a coalescing chain (:512) whose effective value, currentPrice ?? product.CurrentPrice, is exactly the legacy price source — legacy's ProductReservation entity (gba_client/src/entities/products/product.reservation.ts:4-19) has no price field and the legacy carousel shows DataItem.CurrentPrice / pricings-by-agreement (data.item.mini.details.tsx:87-88, product.data.carousel.tsx:321), never a reservation-derived price; (2) the reservations map and stored.reservation have no other consumers (exhaustive grep), so the never-matching find() has zero downstream effect; (3) no scenario yields a wrong displayed number, so this fails the 'demonstrably produces wrong data/behavior' bar. This is console-invented dead code, not an OverLordQty-class silent behavior loss. Crucially, the candidate's proposed fix (read reservation.OrderItem.PricePerItem) would be actively harmful: those OrderItems belong to OTHER sales' current reservations of the product (SQL WHERE at ProductReservationRepository.cs:354-362 filters by product+org+VAT, not by this client's agreement as buyer), so their PricePerItem is another client's order-line price — showing it would create a real divergence from legacy.

rejected.claim: renderPriceExtra unconditionally renders CurrentPrice EUR + CurrentPriceEurToUah UAH (:2009-2010); legacy mini-details for VAT agreements shows CurrentLocalPrice + CurrentPrice and never the EurToUah figure (data.item.mini.details.tsx:52-92 VAT branch, EurToUah only in non-VAT-EUR branch :148-166) — a per-sale-kind gating divergence, not styling.

Sales mix over the last 12 months (VAT-UAH 25,309; non-VAT EUR 79; non-VAT UAH 15; VAT-EUR 2; USD/PLN zero) means every observed sale kind shows numerically identical values to legacy, merely ordered EUR-first; the only divergent kind (VAT-EUR, 2 sales/year) gets an ADDITIONAL correctly-computed current-rate UAH reference where legacy shows a redundant duplicate EUR figure — extra info, not a wrong figure, and the operative EUR price is shown in both UIs. rejected.explanation: Code fact confirmed but no defect: the single-Sale overload CalculatePricingsForSaleWithDynamicPricesWithUsdCalculations (SaleActorsHelpers.cs:654-779) indeed never sets CurrentPriceEurToUah/TotalAmountEurToUah (unlike the plural variant at :600-601/:623-624/:642/:650 and the canonical helper at :459-460/:481-482/:513/:543). However, tracing all nine call sites end-to-end shows the zeroed fields are never observable: BaseSalesGetActor.cs:246/336/447/571/648/738/898 and SalesActor.cs:3231/3267 are all invoice/PZ document-generation handlers that pass the sale only into SalesXlsManager exporters and reply with GenerateSaleInvoiceDocumentResponse file paths (or the doc tuple + bool at SalesActor.cs:3304) — the sale object never leaves the server on these paths. grep for 'EurToUah' across Global.Business.Assistant.Infrastructure.Documents returns 0 matches, so no exporter reads the fields (documents print TotalAmountLocal/CurrentLocalPrice). The fields are EF-ignored and never persisted, so the zeros cannot reach the DB or any client payload. This is a latent copy-of-the-math inconsistency with no wrong data/behavior for any real scenario, and the finding itself concedes the impact is latent.
rejected.fix: Not required (no observable defect). If desired for hygiene when someone later reuses this helper on a client-facing path: mirror the canonical EurToUah computation from SaleActorsHelpers.cs:459-460/:481-482 into both non-USD branches of the single-Sale overload (after :707 and :732), plus the order/sale rollups (Order.TotalAmountEurToUah sum and Sale.TotalAmountEurToUah) as done at :642/:650 — server-side only, in gba-server/src/Global.Business.Assistant.Commercial.Actors/Actors/Sales/SaleActorsHelpers.cs.

The XLSX export (ShipmentListsActor.cs:322 -> SalesShipmentListManager.cs:144) renders zero pricing fields (only recipient/QtyPlaces/comment/phone; CashOnDeliveryAmount is persisted, not computed). The auto endpoint (:411, /sales/shipments/update/filtered/auto) is consumed identically by legacy (warehouse.ukraine.auto.shipments.view.tsx:450, TotalAmount: i.Sale.TotalAmountLocal) and console (ShipmentsTab.tsx totalAmount column, item.Sale.TotalAmountLocal) — both show only TotalAmountLocal; TotalVat/TotalAmountEurToUah are never rendered here. The gate divergence cannot fire because shipment lists only admit Packaging-status sales (SaleRepository.cs:6685 filters SaleLifeCycleType = Packaging), so both versions take the PricePerItem branch. Live dev DB shows no PricePerItem=0 order items on relevant sales (and all 12 ShipmentListItem rows reference deleted sales), so the missing fallback has no real trigger. The shipping exclusion vs the sales pivot is a legacy-era server behavior both legacy views always exhibited — a legacy-faithful quirk, and 2dp-vs-14dp rounding is cosmetic. No scenario demonstrably produces wrong displayed data, so per the verification rules this is code-hygiene duplication, not a data defect.

Note this intentionally changes the auto-view "Вся сума" to include shipping, diverging from long-standing legacy display — only do it as a deliberate product decision, on the server so both clients benefit.

LEGACY PARITY REFERENCE — sales screens beyond pivot header/expand (repo /root/projects/gba_client)

=== 1. SALE EDITING / UPDATE VIEWS ===

1a. "Акт редагування" shift panel (opened from client sales tab / sale list): src/code/modules/managers/sale/modules/clients/components/edit/edit.sale.view.tsx
- Grid row money: `TotalAmount: Math.round(orderItem.TotalAmount*100)/100` with hardcoded `Currency: 'EUR'` (edit.sale.view.tsx:313-314). Qty raw units (:315). No local/UAH amount at all in this grid.
- Shift qty inputs validate `parseInt(value) <= orderItem.Qty && >= 0` and bill+store sum <= Qty (:115-133, :164-182); writes OrderItemBillShiftStatus/OrderItemStoreShiftStatus Qty into `orderItem.ShiftStatuses` then POSTs whole Sale via `ShiftOrderItemAction` (:96).
- "AllInTheCccount" button gated on lifecycle != New (:343-347); "AllToTheWarehouse" always shown (:349).
- Title chosen by lifecycle: New → 'ActForEditingAnAccount' else 'ActForEditingConsignmentNote' (edit.client.sales.view.tsx:114-119).

1b. Sale wizard cart while editing invoice (sale master): src/code/modules/managers/sale/modules/products/shopping.cart/ShoppingCartGrid.tsx
- WithUah predicate = `!Agreement.WithVATAccounting && Currency.Code==='EUR'`; CurrencyCode = 'UAH' when WithUah else agreement code (sale.products.view.tsx:226-233, repeated :244-251, :264-270, :439-445, :504-511, :623-629).
- Row fields: Qty; `Product.CurrentPrice` (EUR unit); local unit = `withUah ? Product.CurrentPriceEurToUah : Product.CurrentLocalPrice` (ShoppingCartGrid.tsx:117); `item.TotalAmount` (EUR); group `Discount` zeroed client-side when Product.Top is 'Х9'/'X9' or IsForZeroSale or IsForSale (:103-105); `item.OneTimeDiscount`; local total = `withUah ? item.TotalAmountEurToUah : item.TotalAmountLocal` (:121).
- Footer totals client-side: Sum(Qty), round(Sum(TotalAmount)) labeled 'EUR', round(Sum(TotalAmountEurToUah)) when withUah else Sum(TotalAmountLocal) labeled currencyCode (:126-134, :146-152).
- Column-header quirk: headers built with `isPoland` (locale) as the withUah arg, not the real withUah prop (:98-99 vs :19,:90) — header text can disagree with the value source; values always follow the prop.

1c. Legacy split/edit cart (SaleProductEditShoppingCart wrapper, sale.products.view.tsx:1317): rows are ShoppingCartItem — Qty, `Product.CurrentPrice`, `Product.CurrentPriceEurToUah`, `OrderItem.TotalAmount`, `OneTimeDiscount`, `OrderItem.TotalAmountEurToUah` — no WithUah switch, always EurToUah (shopping.cart.item.tsx:41-57).
- Current-cart footer: Sum(Qty), round2(Sum(TotalAmount)), round2(Sum(**TotalAmountEurToUah**)) labeled 'TotalAmountInUAH' (sale.product.edit.current.shopping.card.tsx:83-92).
- Future-cart ("НН з рахунку") footer: round2(Sum(**TotalAmountLocal**)) labeled 'TotalAmountInUAH' (sale.product.edit.future.shopping.card.tsx:83-84) — different field than current cart and than its own rows (rows show TotalAmountEurToUah).

=== 2. MERGED SALES UI ===
src/code/modules/managers/sale/modules/merged.sales/ — loads `/sales/get/merged?netId=<sale>` + `GetCurrentUnmergedSaleAction` (api.constants.ts:818, merged.sales.tsx:57-58); saves via `/sales/update/merged` (api.constants.ts:826).

Money fields identical to the regular pivot (sale.item.tsx:228-260: TotalAmountLocal+agreement code / TotalAmountEurToUah+'UAH' when !WithVATAccounting&&EUR, else TotalAmountLocal / TotalAmount+'EUR'; TotalVat only when IsVatSale; positions = OrderItems.length).

Per-item discount badge only when `!HasDiscount && OneTimeDiscount > 0` (:60) — negative item discounts never surfaced here.

=== 5. DISCOUNT MODAL ===
src/code/modules/managers/dashboard/sales.pivot/components/sale.discount.modal.tsx
- Opened via `OPEN_SALE_ONE_TIME_DISCOUNT {Sale, OrderItem?}` (sales.pivot.tsx:1042-1050; reducer sale.reducer.ts:199). OrderItem present ⇒ item-level; absent ⇒ sale-level.

- Read-only when `Sale.BaseLifeCycleStatus.SaleLifeCycleType !== New` (:55, inputs disabled :132,:150, save/cancel hidden :153).
- Validation: percent must satisfy `qty < 100 && qty > -100` on blur (negatives allowed, :102-112); comment REQUIRED (:64-67).
- What it sends: mutates the in-memory Sale then POSTs the WHOLE Sale entity to `/sales/discount/update` (:69-83; epic sale.api.ts:133-149; api.constants.ts:829). Sale-level = writes the same OneTimeDiscount+OneTimeDiscountComment onto EVERY OrderItem plus Sale.OneTimeDiscountComment (:76-80); item-level = only the matched item by NetUid (:70-74).

Item-level badge in expand rows: shown when `!HasDiscount && OneTimeDiscount` truthy (negatives shown here) (sale.expand.content.item.tsx:117-135).
